General guidelines.
   - When you execute commands, always save their logs to files for easier reading if the output becomes large.
   - When running commands, do not set a timeout yourself — let them run as long as needed (default timeout - 2 minutes is more than enough), and once they finish, review the logs in the file.

      Step 5: Report findings with specific errors and line numbers from logs
      This detailed investigation is especially helpful when user mentions CI failures, asks to investigate logs, you see non-passing status, or when finalizing a PR.
      Note: If user says \"failing\" but tools show \"passing\", this might indicate stale data - consider downloading fresh logs and checking timestamps to resolve the discrepancy.
   - When a code or log file has more than 1500 lines, read it in chunks of 1500 lines.
   - When facing a complex problem, do as much tracing as possible and turn on all verbose modes.
   - When you create debug, test, or example/experiment scripts for fixing, always keep them in an ./examples and/or ./experiments folders so you can reuse them later.
   - When testing your assumptions, use the experiment scripts, and add it to ./experiments folder.
   - When your experiments can show real world use case of the software, add it to ./examples folder.
   - When you face something extremely hard, use divide and conquer — it always helps.

Initial research.
   - When you start, make sure you create detailed plan for yourself and follow your todo list step by step, make sure that as many points from these guidelines are added to your todo list to keep track of everything that can help you solve the issue with highest possible quality.
   - When user mentions CI failures or asks to investigate logs, consider adding these todos to track the investigation: (1) List recent CI runs with timestamps, (2) Download logs from failed runs to ci-logs/ directory, (3) Analyze error messages and identify root cause, (4) Implement fix, (5) Verify fix resolves the specific errors found in logs.
   - When you read issue, read all details and comments thoroughly.
   - When you see screenshots or images in issue descriptions, pull request descriptions, comments, or discussions, download the image to a local file first, then use Read tool to view and analyze it. Before reading downloaded images with the Read tool, verify the file is a valid image (not HTML) using a CLI tool like the 'file' command to check the actual file format. When corrupted or non-image files (like GitHub's \"Not Found\" pages saved as .png) are read, they can cause \"Could not process image\" errors and crash the AI solver process. When the file command shows \"HTML\", \"text\", or \"ASCII text\", the download failed — do not call Read on this file. Instead: (1) When images are from GitHub issues/PRs (URLs containing \"github.com/user-attachments\"), these require authentication — retry with: curl -L -H \"Authorization: token \$(gh auth token)\" -o <filename> \"<url>\" (2) When the retry still fails, skip the image and note it was unavailable.

   - When you need related code, use gh search code --owner link-assistant [keywords].

   - When you study related work, study the most recent related pull requests.
   - When issue is not defined enough, write a comment to ask clarifying questions.

   - When you are fixing a bug, please make sure you first find the actual root cause, do as many experiments as needed.
   - When you are fixing a bug and code does not have enough tracing/logs, add them and make sure they stay in the code, but are switched off by default.
   - When you need comments on a pull request, note that GitHub has three different comment types with different API endpoints:
      1. PR review comments (inline code comments): gh api repos/link-assistant/hive-mind/pulls/1525/comments --paginate
      2. PR conversation comments (general discussion): gh api repos/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues/1525/comments --paginate

   - When issue is solvable, first create a test that reproduces the problem, then implement the fix.
   - When implementing features, search for similar existing implementations in the codebase and use them as examples instead of implementing everything from scratch.

      write unit tests with mocks for easy and quick start.

Reproducible testing.
   - When fixing a bug, create a test that reproduces the problem before implementing the fix. When you cannot reproduce the problem, you cannot verify the fix.
   - When encountering logic bugs, write an automated test that fails due to the bug, then implement the fix to make it pass.
   - When encountering UI bugs, capture a screenshot showing the problem state, then create a visual regression test or manual verification screenshot after the fix.
   - When creating tests, prefer minimum reproducible examples - the simplest test case that demonstrates the issue.
   - When submitting a fix, include in the PR description: (1) how to reproduce the issue, (2) the automated test that verifies the fix, (3) before/after screenshots for UI issues.
   - When a bug fix doesn't have a reproducing test, the fix is incomplete - regressions can silently occur later.

execCommandAsync(fallback);\n      if (result) break;\n    }\n  }\n\n  if (verbose && result) {\n    console.log(`[VERBOSE] ${cmdDef.key}: ${result}`);\n  } else if (verbose && !result) {\n Rust ecosystem\\n');\n\ntest('parseVersion: rustc with commit and date', () => {\n  const result = parseVersion('rust', 'rustc 1.94.1 (e408947bf 2026-03-25)');\n  assert.equal(result, '1.94.1 (e408947bf, 2026-03-25)');\n});\n\ntest('parseVersion: cargo with commit and date', () => {\n  const result = parseVersion('cargo', 'cargo 1.94.1 (29ea6fb6a 2026-03-24)');\n  assert.equal(result, '1.94.1 (29ea6fb6a, 2026-03-24)');\n});\n\n// ============================================================================\n// parseVersion Tests — Go\n// ============================================================================\n\nconsole.log('\\n📋 parseVersion - Ruby\\n');\n\ntest('parseVersion: ruby with revision and platform', () => {\n  const result = parseVersion('ruby', 'ruby 3.4.9 (2026-03-11 revision 76cca827ab) +PRISM [x86_64-linux]');\n  assert.equal(result, '3.4.9 (2026-03-11 revision 76cca827ab, +PRISM [x86_64-linux])');\n});\n\ntest('parseVersion: rbenv', () => {\n  assert.equal(parseVersion('rbenv', 'rbenv 1.3.2-20-g23c3041'), '1.3.2-20-g23c3041');\n});\n\n// ============================================================================\n// parseVersion Tests — Kotlin / Swift / R\n// ============================================================================\n\nconsole.log('\\n📋 parseVersion - Development tools\\n');\n\ntest('parseVersion: git', () => {\n  assert.equal(parseVersion('git', 'git version 2.43.0'), '2.43.0');\n});\n\ntest('parseVersion: gh with date', () => {\n  assert.equal(parseVersion('gh', 'gh version 2.89.0 (2026-03-26)'), '2.89.0 (2026-03-26)');\n});\n\ntest('parseVersion: glab', () => {\n  assert.equal(parseVersion('glab', 'glab version 1.36.0'), '1.36.0');\n});\n\ntest('parseVersion: curl with platform', () => {\n  assert.equal(parseVersion('curl', 'curl 8.19.0 (x86_64-pc-linux-gnu) libcurl/8.19.0'), '8.19.0 (x86_64-pc-linux-gnu)');\n});\n\ntest('parseVersion: wget', () => {\n  assert.equal(parseVersion('wget', 'GNU Wget 1.21.4 built on linux-gnu.'), '1.21.4');\n});\n\ntest('parseVersion: screen with GNU and date', () => {\n  assert.equal(parseVersion('screen', 'Screen version 4.09.01 (GNU) 20-Aug-23'), '4.09.01 (GNU, 20-Aug-23)');\n});\n\ntest('parseVersion: expect', () => {\n  assert.equal(parseVersion('expect', 'expect version 5.45.4'), '5.45.4');\n});\n\ntest('parseVersion: zip with date', () => {\n  assert.equal(parseVersion('zip', 'This is Zip 3.0 (July 5th 2008), by Info-ZIP.'), '3.0 (July 5th 2008)');\n});\n\ntest('parseVersion: unzip with date', () => {\n  assert.equal(parseVersion('unzip', 'UnZip 6.00 of 20 April 2009, by Debian. Original by Info-ZIP.'), '6.00 (20 April 2009)');\n});\n\ntest('parseVersion: homebrew', () => {\n  assert.equal(parseVersion('brew', 'Homebrew 5.1.2'), '5.1.2');\n});\n\n// ============================================================================\n// parseVersion Tests — Xvfb (dpkg format)\n// ============================================================================\n\nconsole.log('\\n📋 parseVersion - Xvfb\\n');\n\ntest('parseVersion: xvfb from dpkg output', () => {\n  const result = parseVersion('xvfb', \"ii  xvfb           2:21.1.12-1ubuntu1.5 amd64        Virtual Framebuffer 'fake' X server\");\n  assert.equal(result, '21.1.12-1ubuntu1.5');\n});\n\ntest('parseVersion: xvfb X.Org format', () => {\n  const result = parseVersion('xvfb', 'X.Org X Server 1.21.1.4');\n  assert.equal(result, '1.21.1.4');\n});\n\n// ============================================================================\n// parseVersion Tests — OCaml/Lean ecosystem\n// ============================================================================\n\nconsole.log('\\n📋 parseVersion - OCaml, Lean\\n');\n\ntest('parseVersion: ocaml', () => {\n  assert.equal(parseVersion('ocaml', 'The OCaml toplevel, version 5.4.1'), '5.4.1');\n});\n\ntest('parseVersion: rocq', () => {\n  assert.equal(parseVersion('rocq', 'The Rocq Prover, version 9.1.1'), '9.1.1');\n});\n\ntest('parseVersion: elan with commit and date', () => {\n  const result = parseVersion('elan', 'elan 4.2.1 (3d5138e15 2026-03-18)');\n  assert.equal(result, '4.2.1 (3d5138e15, 2026-03-18)');\n});\n\ntest('parseVersion: lean with platform and commit', () => {\n  const result = parseVersion('lean', 'Lean (version 4.29.0, x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu, commit 98dc76e3c0a9b856c9b98726b713fb04fab16740, Release)');\n  assert.equal(result, '4.29.0 (x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu, commit 98dc76e3c0a9b856c9b98726b713fb04fab16740, Release)');\n});\n\n// ============================================================================\n// parseVersion Tests — AI agents and browsers\n// ============================================================================\n\nconsole.log('\\n📋 parseVersion - AI agents, browsers\\n');\n\ntest('parseVersion: claude code', () => {\n  assert.equal(parseVersion('claudeCode', '2.1.87 (Claude Code)'), '2.1.87 (Claude Code)');\n});\n\ntest('parseVersion: copilot strips trailing dot', () => {\n  assert.equal(parseVersion('copilot', 'GitHub Copilot CLI 1.0.14.'), '1.0.14');\n});\n\ntest('parseVersion: deno with build info', () => {\n  const result = parseVersion('deno', 'deno 2.7.9 (stable, release, x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu)');\n  assert.equal(result, '2.7.9 (stable, release, x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu)');\n});\n\ntest('parseVersion: playwright', () => {\n  assert.equal(parseVersion('playwright', 'Version 1.58.2'), '1.58.2');\n});\n\ntest('parseVersion: playwright mcp from npm list', () => {\n  assert.equal(parseVersion('playwrightMcp', '`-- @playwright/mcp@0.0.69'), '0.0.69');\n});\n\ntest('parseVersion: chrome', () => {\n  assert.equal(parseVersion('chrome', 'Google Chrome 146.0.7680.164'), '146.0.7680.164');\n});\n\ntest('parseVersion: msedge', () => {\n  assert.equal(parseVersion('msedge', 'Microsoft Edge 146.0.3856.84'), '146.0.3856.84');\n});\n\ntest('parseVersion: perlbrew from full path', () => {\n  assert.equal(parseVersion('perlbrew', '/workspace/.perl5/bin/perlbrew  - Edge cases\\n');\n\ntest('parseVersion: null input returns null', () => {\n  assert.equal(parseVersion('rust', null), null);\n});\n\ntest('parseVersion: empty string returns empty', () => {\n  assert.equal(parseVersion('rust', ''), '');\n});\n\ntest('parseVersion: unknown key returns raw string', () => {\n  assert.equal(parseVersion('unknownTool', 'some output'), 'some output');\n});\n\ntest('parseVersion: non-matching output returns raw string', () => {\n  assert.equal(parseVersion('rust', 'totally unexpected output'), 'totally unexpected output');\n});\n\n// ============================================================================\n// formatVersionMessage Tests — Playwright MCP new format (Issue #1514)\n// ============================================================================\n\nconsole.log('\\n📋 formatVersionMessage - `19${dmy[3]}` : `20${dmy[3]}`;\n      return `${fullYear}-${month}-${dmy[1].padStart(2, '0')}`;\n    }\n  }\n\n  // \"DD Month YYYY\" (e.g. \"20 April 2009\")\n  const dmY = s.match(/^(\\d{1,2})\\s+([A-Za-z]+)\\s+(\\d{4})$/);\n  if (dmY) {\n    const month = MONTH_MAP[dmY[2].toLowerCase()];\n    if (month) return `${dmY[3]}-${month}-${dmY[1].padStart(2, '0')}`;\n  }\n\n  // \"Month DDth YYYY\" or \"Month DD YYYY\" (e.g. \"July 5th 2008\", \"Jan 13 2026\")\n  const mdY = s.match(/^([A-Za-z]+)\\s+(\\d{1,2})(?:st|nd|rd|th)?\\s+(\\d{4})$/);\n  if (mdY) {\n    const month = MONTH_MAP[mdY[1].toLowerCase()];\n    if (month) return `${mdY[3]}-${month}-${mdY[2].padStart(2, '0')}`;\n  }\n\n  // \"Month DD YYYY HH:MM:SS\" (e.g. \"Jan 13 2026 22:36:55\")\n  const mdYt = s.match(/^([A-Za-z]+)\\s+(\\d{1,2})\\s+(\\d{4})\\s+(\\d{2}:\\d{2}:\\d{2})$/);\n  if (mdYt) {\n    const month = MONTH_MAP[mdYt[1].toLowerCase()];\n    if (month) return `${mdYt[3]}-${month}-${mdYt[2].padStart(2, '0')} ${mdYt[4]}`;\n  }\n\n  return s;\n}\n\n/**\n * Strip OS/architecture info from a version extra string.\n * Removes patterns like \"x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu\", \"linux/amd64\", \"[x86_64-linux]\", etc.\n * @param {string} s - String to clean\n * @returns {string} Cleaned string\n */\nfunction stripArchInfo(s) {\n  if (!s) return s;\n  return s\n    // Remove bracketed arch like [x86_64-linux]\n    .replace(/\\[[\\w-]+\\]\\s*/g, '')\n    // Remove triple-target patterns like x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu, x86_64-pc-linux-gnu\n    .replace(/\\b\\w+[-_]\\w+[-_]\\w+[-_]\\w+\\b/g, '')\n    // Remove platform/arch patterns like linux/amd64\n    .replace(/\\b(?:linux|darwin|win32|windows)\\/\\w+\\b/gi, '')\n    .trim();\n}\n\n/**\n * Execute a command asynchronously and return its output, or null if it fails"

    "originalFile": "#!/usr/bin/env node\n\n// Version information library for hive-mind project\n// Provides comprehensive version information for bot, commands, and runtime\n//\n// Performance optimization (issue #1320):\n// Uses Promise.all for parallel command execution instead of sequential execSync.\n// This reduces version gathering time from ~30-150s to ~5s (limited by slowest command).\n\nimport { getVersion } from './version.lib.mjs';\nimport { exec } from 'child_process';\nimport { promisify } from 'util';\n\nconst execAsync = promisify(exec);\n\n/**\n * Execute a command asynchronously and return its output, or null if it fails\n * @param {string} command - Command to execute\n * @param {number} timeout - Timeout in milliseconds (default: 5000ms)\n * @returns {Promise<string|null>} Command output or null\n */\nasync function execCommandAsync(command, timeout = 5000) {\n  try {\n    const { stdout } = await execAsync(command, { timeout, maxBuffer: 1024 * 1024 });\n    const trimmed = stdout.trim();\n    // Return null if the output looks like an error message\n    if (trimmed.includes('not found') || trimmed.includes('command not found') || trimmed === '') {\n      return null;\n    }\n    return trimmed;\n  } catch {\n    return null;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Per-tool regex parsers to normalize raw --version output into uniform format:\n *   <version> (<commit>, <revision>, <date>, etc.)\n *\n * Each parser returns { version, extra[] } or null if it doesn't match.\n * The `version` is the most specific version string (for bug reporting).\n * Items in `extra` are joined with \", \" and placed in parentheses.\n *\n * @type {Record<string, (raw: string) => {version: string, extra: string[]} | null>}\n */\nconst VERSION_PARSERS = {\n  // rustc 1.94.1 (e408947bf 2026-03-25)\n  rust: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^rustc\\s+([\\d.]+(?:-\\S+)?)\\s*(?:\\(([^)]+)\\))?/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    const extra = m[2] ? m[2].trim().split(/\\s+/) : [];\n    return { version: m[1], extra };\n  },\n  // cargo 1.94.1 (29ea6fb6a 2026-03-24)\n  cargo: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^cargo\\s+([\\d.]+(?:-\\S+)?)\\s*(?:\\(([^)]+)\\))?/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    const extra = m[2] ? m[2].trim().split(/\\s+/) : [];\n    return { version: m[1], extra };\n  },\n  // go version go1.26.1 linux/amd64\n  go: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/go([\\d.]+(?:\\S*)?)\\s+(.*)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [m[2].trim()] };\n  },\n  // PHP 8.3.30 (cli) (built: Jan 13 2026 22:36:55) (NTS)\n  php: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^PHP\\s+([\\d.]+(?:-\\S+)?)\\s*(.*)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    const tags = [];\n    const parts = m[2].matchAll(/\\(([^)]+)\\)/g);\n    for (const p of parts) tags.push(p[1]);\n    return { version: m[1], extra: tags };\n  },\n  // openjdk version \"21\" 2023-09-19 LTS\n  java: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/version\\s+\"([^\"]+)\"(?:\\s+(.+))?/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: m[2] ? [m[2].trim()] : [] };\n  },\n  // gcc (Ubuntu 13.3.0-6ubuntu2~24.04.1) 13.3.0 — use full distro version\n  gcc: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^gcc\\s+(?:\\((\\S+)\\s+([\\d.]+\\S*)\\)\\s+)?([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    // If distro info present, use full distro version (e.g. 13.3.0-6ubuntu2~24.04.1)\n    if (m[1] && m[2]) return { version: m[2], extra: [] };\n    return { version: m[3], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // g++ (Ubuntu 13.3.0-6ubuntu2~24.04.1) 13.3.0 — use full distro version\n  gpp: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^g\\+\\+\\s+(?:\\((\\S+)\\s+([\\d.]+\\S*)\\)\\s+)?([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    // If distro info present, use full distro version (e.g. 13.3.0-6ubuntu2~24.04.1)\n    if (m[1] && m[2]) return { version: m[2], extra: [] };\n    return { version: m[3], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // clang version 17.0.0 (https://github.com/... commit)\n  clang: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^clang\\s+version\\s+([\\d.]+(?:-\\S+)?)\\s*(?:\\(([^)]+)\\))?/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: m[2] ? [m[2].trim()] : [] };\n  },\n  // ii  xvfb  2:21.1.12-1ubuntu1.5  amd64  Virtual Framebuffer...\n  xvfb: raw => {\n    // dpkg output format\n    const dpkg = raw.match(/^ii\\s+xvfb\\s+(\\S+)/);\n    if (dpkg) {\n      // Strip epoch (e.g. \"2:21.1.12-1ubuntu1.5\" -> \"21.1.12-1ubuntu1.5\")\n      const ver = dpkg[1].replace(/^\\d+:/, '');\n      return { version: ver, extra: [] };\n    }\n    // // Xvfb returns \"Unrecognized option: -version\" — this is handled by fixing the command\n  // to use dpkg fallback first\n\n  // agent 1.0.0 or similar\n  agent: raw => {\n    const m = (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // opencode 1.3.10 or similar\n  opencode: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // qwen-code version\n  qwenCode: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // gemini version\n  gemini: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n};\n\n/**\n * Parse a raw version string using the per-tool parser, returning uniform format:\n *   \"<version>\" or \"<version> (<extra1>, <extra2>, ...)\"\n * Falls back to the raw string if no parser matches.\n * @param {string} key - Tool key (must match a key in VERSION_PARSERS)\n * @param {string} raw - Raw version string from command output\n * @returns {string} Parsed version string in uniform format\n */\nexport function parseVersion(key, raw) {\n  if (!raw) return raw;\n  const parser = VERSION_PARSERS[key];\n  if (!parser) return raw;\n  const result = parser(raw);\n  if (!result) return raw;\n  const { version, extra } = result;\n  if (extra && extra.length > 0) {\n    return `${version} (${extra.join(', ')})`;\n  }\n  return version;\n}\n\n/**\n * Command definitions for version checking\n * Each entry has: key, command, and optional fallbacks\n * @type {Array<{key: string, command: string, fallbacks?: string[]}>}\n */\nconst VERSION_COMMANDS = [\n  // AI Agents and Tools (--tool options)\n  { key: 'claudeCode', command: 'claude --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'agent', command: 'agent --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'codex', command: 'codex --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'opencode', command: 'opencode --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'qwenCode', command: 'qwen-code --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'gemini', command: 'gemini --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'copilot', command: 'copilot --version 2>&1' },\n\n  // Browser Automation\n  { key: 'playwright', command: 'playwright --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'playwrightTest', command: \"npm list -g @playwright/test --depth=0 2>&1 | grep @playwright/test | awk '{print $2}'\" },\n  { key: 'playwrightMcp', command: \"npm list -g @playwright/mcp --depth=0 2>&1 | grep @playwright/mcp | awk '{print $2}'\" },\n  { key: 'playwrightMcpStatus', command: 'timeout 5 claude mcp list 2>&1 | grep -i playwright | head -1' },\n  { key: 'puppeteerBrowsers', command: \"npm list -g @puppeteer/browsers --depth=0 2>&1 | grep @puppeteer/browsers | awk '{print $2}'\" },\n\n  // Browsers (installed via Playwright)\n  { key: 'chrome', command: 'google-chrome --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'chromium', command: 'chromium --version 2>&1', fallbacks: ['chromium-browser --version 2>&1', \"ls ~/.cache/ms-playwright/ 2>/dev/null | grep -oE 'chromium-[0-9]+' | head -1\"] },\n  { key: 'firefox', command: 'firefox --version 2>&1', fallbacks: [\"ls ~/.cache/ms-playwright/ 2>/dev/null | grep -oE 'firefox-[0-9]+' | head -1\"] },\n  { key: 'msedge', command: 'microsoft-edge --version 2>&1', fallbacks: ['microsoft-edge-stable --version 2>&1'] },\n  { key: 'webkit', command: \"ls ~/.cache/ms-playwright/ 2>/dev/null | grep -oE 'webkit-[0-9]+' | head -1\" },\n\n  // JavaScript/Node.js ecosystem\n  { key: 'bun', command: 'bun --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'deno', command: 'deno --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'npm', command: 'npm --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'nvm', command: 'nvm --version 2>&1' },\n\n  // Python ecosystem\n  { key: 'python', command: 'python --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'pyenv', command: 'pyenv --version 2>&1' },\n\n  // Rust ecosystem\n  { key: 'rust', command: 'rustc --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'cargo', command: 'cargo --version 2>&1' },\n\n  // Java ecosystem\n  { key: 'java', command: 'java -version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'sdkman', command: \"sdk version 2>&1 | grep -oE '[0-9]+\\\\.[0-9]+\\\\.[0-9]+'\" },\n\n  // Go\n  { key: 'go', command: 'go version 2>&1' },\n\n  // PHP\n  { key: 'php', command: 'php --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n\n  // .NET\n  { key: 'dotnet', command: 'dotnet --version 2>&1' },\n\n  // Perl ecosystem\n  { key: 'perl', command: \"perl -v 2>&1 | grep -oE 'v[0-9]+\\\\.[0-9]+\\\\.[0-9]+'\" },\n  { key: 'perlbrew', command: 'perlbrew --version 2>&1' },\n\n  // OCaml/Rocq ecosystem\n  { key: 'ocaml', command: 'ocaml --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'opam', command: 'opam --version 2>&1' },\n  // Rocq has fallback commands (rocq -> rocqc -> coqc)\n  { key: 'rocq', command: 'rocq -v 2>&1 | head -n1', fallbacks: ['rocqc --version 2>&1 | head -n1', 'coqc --version 2>&1 | head -n1'] },\n\n  // Lean ecosystem\n  { key: 'lean', command: 'lean --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'elan', command: 'elan --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'lake', command: 'lake --version 2>&1' },\n\n  // C/C++ Development Tools\n  { key: 'gcc', command: 'gcc --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'gpp', command: 'g++ --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'clang', command: 'clang --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'llvm', command: 'llvm-config --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'lld', command: 'ld.lld --version 2>&1 | head -n1', fallbacks: ['lld --version 2>&1 | head -n1'] },\n  { key: 'make', command: 'make --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'cmake', command: 'cmake --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n\n  // Ruby ecosystem\n  { key: 'ruby', command: 'ruby --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'rbenv', command: 'rbenv --version 2>&1' },\n\n  // Kotlin\n  { key: 'kotlin', command: 'kotlin -version 2>&1' },\n\n  // Swift\n  { key: 'swift', command: 'swift --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n\n  // R\n  { key: 'r', command: 'R --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n\n  // Development Tools\n  { key: 'git', command: 'git --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'gh', command: 'gh --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'glab', command: 'glab --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'brew', command: 'brew --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'nasm', command: 'nasm --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'fasm', command: 'fasm 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'curl', command: 'curl --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'wget', command: 'wget --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'zip', command: 'zip --version 2>&1 | head -n2 | tail -n1' },\n  { key: 'unzip', command: 'unzip -v 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'expect', command: 'expect -version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'screen', command: 'screen --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'xvfb', command: 'dpkg -l xvfb 2>/dev/null | grep \"^ii\" | head -1', fallbacks: ['Xvfb -version 2>&1 | head -n1'] },\n];\n\n/**\n * Execute a version command with optional fallbacks\n * @param {{key: string, command: string, fallbacks?: string[]}} cmdDef - Command definition\n * @param {boolean} verbose - Enable verbose logging\n * @returns {Promise<{key: string, value: string|null}>}\n */\nasync function executeVersionCommand(cmdDef, verbose) {\n  let result = await execCommandAsync(cmdDef.command);\n\n  // Try fallbacks if primary command failed\n  if (!result && cmdDef.fallbacks) {\n    for (const fallback of cmdDef.fallbacks) {\n      result = await execCommandAsync(fallback);\n      if (result) break;\n    }\n  }\n\n  if (verbose && result) {\n    console.log(`[VERBOSE] ${cmdDef.key}: ${result}`);\n  } else if (verbose && !result) {\n    console.log(`[VERBOSE] ${cmdDef.key}: not found`);\n  }\n\n  return { key: cmdDef.key, value: result };\n}\n\n/**\n * Get comprehensive version information for all components\n * Uses Promise.all for parallel execution (issue #1320)\n * @param {boolean} verbose - Enable verbose logging\n * @param {string} [processVersion] - Optional: version from the running process (for restart warning)\n * @returns {Promise<Object>} Version information object\n */\nexport async function getVersionInfo(verbose = false, processVersion = null) {\n  const startTime = Date.now();\n\n  try {\n    if (verbose) {\n      console.log('[VERBOSE] Gathering version information (parallel execution)...');\n    }\n\n    // Get hive-mind package version\n    const packageVersion = await getVersion();\n    if (verbose) {\n      console.log(`[VERBOSE] Package version: ${packageVersion}`);\n    }\n\n    // Execute all version commands in parallel\n    const results = await Promise.all(VERSION_COMMANDS.map(cmd => executeVersionCommand(cmd, verbose)));\n\n    // Convert results array to object\n    const versions = {};\n    for (const { key, value } of results) {\n      versions[key] = value;\n    }\n\n    // Add Node.js version (always available from process)\n    versions.node = process.version;\n    if (verbose) {\n      console.log(`[VERBOSE] Node.js version: ${versions.node}`);\n    }\n\n    // Platform information\n    const platform = process.platform;\n    const arch = process.arch;\n    versions.platform = `${platform} (${arch})`;\n    if (verbose) {\n      console.log(`[VERBOSE] Platform: ${versions.platform}`);\n    }\n\n    // Check if process version differs from installed version (restart warning)\n    const needsRestart = processVersion && processVersion !== packageVersion;\n\n    // Build version info object\n    const versionInfo = {\n      success: true,\n      versions: {\n        // Hive-mind package (single entry, not duplicated)\n        hiveMind: packageVersion,\n        processVersion: processVersion || packageVersion,\n        needsRestart,\n\n        // AI Agents (--tool options)\n        claudeCode:      // Performance metrics\n      gatherTimeMs: Date.now() - startTime,\n    };\n\n    if (verbose) {\n      console.log(`[VERBOSE] Version info gathered in ${versionInfo.gatherTimeMs}ms`);\n      console.log('[VERBOSE] Version info:', JSON.stringify(versionInfo, null, 2));\n    }\n\n    return versionInfo;\n  } catch (error) {\n    if (verbose) {\n      console.error('[VERBOSE] Error gathering           " const execAsync = promisify(exec);",

          " /**",
          "+ * Month name/abbreviation to zero-padded number mapping",
          "+ */",
          "+const MONTH_MAP = {",
          "+  jan: '01', january: '01',",
          "+  feb: '02', february: '02',",
          "+  mar: '03', march: '03',",
          "+  apr: '04', april: '04',",
          "+  may: '05',",
          "+  jun: '06', june: '06',",
          "+  jul: '07', july: '07',",
          "+  aug: '08', august: '08',",
          "+  sep: '09', september: '09',",
          "+  oct: '10', october: '10',",
          "+  nov: '11', november: '11',",
          "+  dec: '12', december: '12',",
          "+};",
          "+",
          "+/**",
          "+ * Normalize a date string to ISO format (YYYY-MM-DD).",
          "+ * Handles formats like:",
          "+ *   \"20-Aug-23\"       → \"2023-08-20\"",
          "+ *   \"20 April 2009\"   → \"2009-04-20\"",
          "+ *   \"July 5th 2008\"   → \"2008-07-05\"",
          "+ *   \"Jan 13 2026\"     → \"2026-01-13\"",
          "+ *   \"2024-02-29\"      → \"2024-02-29\" (passthrough)",
          "+ * Returns the original string if parsing fails.",
          "+ * @param {string} dateStr - Date string to normalize",
          "+ * @returns {string} ISO date string or original",
          "+ */",
          "+export function normalizeDate(dateStr) {",
          "+  if (!dateStr) return dateStr;",
          "+  const s = dateStr.trim();",
          "+",
          "+  // Already ISO: YYYY-MM-DD",
          "+  if (/^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}$/.test(s)) return s;",
          "+",
          "+  // \"DD-Mon-YY\" (e.g. \"20-Aug-23\")",
          "+  const dmy = s.match(/^(\\d{1,2})-([A-Za-z]{3})-(\\d{2})$/);",
          "+  if (dmy) {",
          "+    const month = MONTH_MAP[dmy[2].toLowerCase()];",
          "+    if (month) {",
          "+      const year = parseInt(dmy[3], 10);",
          "+      const fullYear = year >= 70 ? `19${dmy[3]}` : `20${dmy[3]}`;",
          "+      return `${fullYear}-${month}-${dmy[1].padStart(2, '0')}`;",
          "+    }",
          "+  }",
          "+",
          "+  // \"DD Month YYYY\" (e.g. \"20 April 2009\")",
          "+  const dmY = s.match(/^(\\d{1,2})\\s+([A-Za-z]+)\\s+(\\d{4})$/);",
          "+  if (dmY) {",
          "+    const month = MONTH_MAP[dmY[2].toLowerCase()];",
          "+    if (month) return `${dmY[3]}-${month}-${dmY[1].padStart(2, '0')}`;",
          "+  }",
          "+",
          "+  // \"Month DDth YYYY\" or \"Month DD YYYY\" (e.g. \"July 5th 2008\", \"Jan 13 2026\")",
          "+  const mdY = s.match(/^([A-Za-z]+)\\s+(\\d{1,2})(?:st|nd|rd|th)?\\s+(\\d{4})$/);",
          "+  if (mdY) {",
          "+    const month = MONTH_MAP[mdY[1].toLowerCase()];",
          "+    if (month) return `${mdY[3]}-${month}-${mdY[2].padStart(2, '0')}`;",
          "+  }",
          "+",
          "+  // \"Month DD YYYY HH:MM:SS\" (e.g. \"Jan 13 2026 22:36:55\")",
          "+  const mdYt = s.match(/^([A-Za-z]+)\\s+(\\d{1,2})\\s+(\\d{4})\\s+(\\d{2}:\\d{2}:\\d{2})$/);",
          "+  if (mdYt) {",
          "+    const month = MONTH_MAP[mdYt[1].toLowerCase()];",
          "+    if (month) return `${mdYt[3]}-${month}-${mdYt[2].padStart(2, '0')} ${mdYt[4]}`;",
          "+  }",
          "+",
          "+  return s;",
          "+}",
          "+",
          "+/**",
          "+ * Strip OS/architecture info from a version extra string.",
          "+ * Removes patterns like \"x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu\", \"linux/amd64\", \"[x86_64-linux]\", etc.",
          "+ * @param {string} s - String to clean",
          "+ * @returns {string} Cleaned string",
          "+ */",
          "+function stripArchInfo(s) {",
          "+  if (!s) return s;",
          "+  return s",
          "+    // Remove bracketed arch like [x86_64-linux]",
          "+    .replace(/\\[[\\w-]+\\]\\s*/g, '')",
          "+    // Remove triple-target patterns like x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu, x86_64-pc-linux-gnu",
          "+    .replace(/\\b\\w+[-_]\\w+[-_]\\w+[-_]\\w+\\b/g, '')",
          "+    // Remove platform/arch patterns like linux/amd64",
          "+    .replace(/\\b(?:linux|darwin|win32|windows)\\/\\w+\\b/gi, '')",
          "+    .trim();",
          "+}",
          "+",
          "+/**",
          "  * Execute a command asynchronously and return its output, or null if it fails",
          "  * @param {string} command - Command to execute",
          "  * @param {number} timeout - Timeout in milliseconds (default: 5000ms)"
        ]

    "newString": "const execAsync = promisify(exec);\n\n/**\n * Month name/abbreviation to zero-padded number mapping\n */\nconst MONTH_MAP = {\n  jan: '01', january: '01',\n  feb: '02', february: '02',\n  mar: '03', march: '03',\n  apr: '04', april: '04',\n  may: '05',\n  jun: '06', june: '06',\n  jul: '07', july: '07',\n  aug: '08', august: '08',\n  sep: '09', september: '09',\n  oct: '10', october: '10',\n  nov: '11', november: '11',\n  dec: '12', december: '12',\n};\n\n/**\n * Normalize a date string to ISO format (YYYY-MM-DD).\n * Handles formats like:\n *   \"20-Aug-23\"       → \"2023-08-20\"\n *   \"20 April 2009\"   → \"2009-04-20\"\n *   \"July 5th 2008\"   → \"2008-07-05\"\n *   \"Jan 13 2026\"     → \"2026-01-13\"\n *   \"2024-02-29\"      → \"2024-02-29\" (passthrough)\n * Returns the original string if parsing fails.\n * @param {string} dateStr - Date string to normalize\n * @returns {string} ISO date string or original\n */\nexport function normalizeDate(dateStr) {\n  if (!dateStr) return dateStr;\n  const s = dateStr.trim();\n\n  // Already ISO: YYYY-MM-DD\n  if (/^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}$/.test(s)) return s;\n\n  // \"DD-Mon-YY\" (e.g. \"20-Aug-23\")\n  const dmy = s.match(/^(\\d{1,2})-([A-Za-z]{3})-(\\d{2})$/);\n  if (dmy) {\n    const month = MONTH_MAP[dmy[2].toLowerCase()];\n    if (month) {\n      const year = parseInt(dmy[3], 10);\n      const fullYear = year >= 70 ? `19${dmy[3]}` : `20${dmy[3]}`;\n      return `${fullYear}-${month}-${dmy[1].padStart(2, '0')}`;\n    }\n  }\n\n  // \"DD Month YYYY\" (e.g. \"20 April 2009\")\n  const dmY = s.match(/^(\\d{1,2})\\s+([A-Za-z]+)\\s+(\\d{4})$/);\n  if (dmY) {\n    const month = MONTH_MAP[dmY[2].toLowerCase()];\n    if (month) return `${dmY[3]}-${month}-${dmY[1].padStart(2, '0')}`;\n  }\n\n  // \"Month DDth YYYY\" or \"Month DD YYYY\" (e.g. \"July 5th 2008\", \"Jan 13 2026\")\n  const mdY = s.match(/^([A-Za-z]+)\\s+(\\d{1,2})(?:st|nd|rd|th)?\\s+(\\d{4})$/);\n  if (mdY) {\n    const month = MONTH_MAP[mdY[1].toLowerCase()];\n    if (month) return `${mdY[3]}-${month}-${mdY[2].padStart(2, '0')}`;\n  }\n\n  // \"Month DD YYYY HH:MM:SS\" (e.g. \"Jan 13 2026 22:36:55\")\n  const mdYt = s.match(/^([A-Za-z]+)\\s+(\\d{1,2})\\s+(\\d{4})\\s+(\\d{2}:\\d{2}:\\d{2})$/);\n  if (mdYt) {\n    const month = MONTH_MAP[mdYt[1].toLowerCase()];\n    if (month) return `${mdYt[3]}-${month}-${mdYt[2].padStart(2, '0')} ${mdYt[4]}`;\n  }\n\n  return s;\n}\n\n/**\n * Strip OS/architecture info from a version extra string.\n * Removes patterns like \"x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu\", \"linux/amd64\", \"[x86_64-linux]\", etc.\n * @param {string} s - String to clean\n * @returns {string} Cleaned string\n */\nfunction stripArchInfo(s) {\n  if (!s) return s;\n  return s\n    // Remove bracketed arch like [x86_64-linux]\n    .replace(/\\[[\\w-]+\\]\\s*/g, '')\n    // Remove triple-target patterns like x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu, x86_64-pc-linux-gnu\n    .replace(/\\b\\w+[-_]\\w+[-_]\\w+[-_]\\w+\\b/g, '')\n    // Remove platform/arch patterns like linux/amd64\n    .replace(/\\b(?:linux|darwin|win32|windows)\\/\\w+\\b/gi, '')\n    .trim();\n}\n\n/**\n * Execute a command asynchronously and return its output, or null if it fails",
    "originalFile": "#!/usr/bin/env node\n\n// Version information library for hive-mind project\n// Provides comprehensive version information for bot, commands, and runtime\n//\n// Performance optimization (issue #1320):\n// Uses Promise.all for parallel command execution instead of sequential execSync.\n// This reduces version gathering time from ~30-150s to ~5s (limited by slowest command).\n\nimport { getVersion } from './version.lib.mjs';\nimport { exec } from 'child_process';\nimport { promisify } from 'util';\n\nconst execAsync = promisify(exec);\n\n/**\n * Execute a command asynchronously and return its output, or null if it fails\n * @param {string} command - Command to execute\n * @param {number} timeout - Timeout in milliseconds (default: 5000ms)\n * @returns {Promise<string|null>} Command output or null\n */\nasync function execCommandAsync(command, timeout = 5000) {\n  try {\n    const { stdout } = await execAsync(command, { timeout, maxBuffer: 1024 * 1024 });\n    const trimmed = stdout.trim();\n    // Return null if the output looks like an error message\n    if (trimmed.includes('not found') || trimmed.includes('command not found') || trimmed === '') {\n      return null;\n    }\n    return trimmed;\n  } catch {\n    return null;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Per-tool regex parsers to normalize raw --version output into uniform format:\n *   <version> (<commit>, <revision>, <date>, etc.)\n *\n * Each parser returns { version, extra[] } or null if it doesn't match.\n * The `version` is the most specific version string (for bug reporting).\n * Items in `extra` are joined with \", \" and placed in parentheses.\n *\n * @type {Record<string, (raw: string) => {version: string, extra: string[]} | null>}\n */\nconst VERSION_PARSERS = {\n  // rustc 1.94.1 (e408947bf 2026-03-25)\n  rust: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^rustc\\s+([\\d.]+(?:-\\S+)?)\\s*(?:\\(([^)]+)\\))?/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    const extra = m[2] ? m[2].trim().split(/\\s+/) : [];\n    return { version: m[1], extra };\n  },\n  // cargo 1.94.1 (29ea6fb6a 2026-03-24)\n  cargo: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^cargo\\s+([\\d.]+(?:-\\S+)?)\\s*(?:\\(([^)]+)\\))?/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    const extra = m[2] ? m[2].trim().split(/\\s+/) : [];\n    return { version: m[1], extra };\n  },\n  // go version go1.26.1 linux/amd64\n  go: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/go([\\d.]+(?:\\S*)?)\\s+(.*)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [m[2].trim()] };\n  },\n  // PHP 8.3.30 (cli) (built: Jan 13 2026 22:36:55) (NTS)\n  php: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^PHP\\s+([\\d.]+(?:-\\S+)?)\\s*(.*)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    const tags = [];\n    const parts = m[2].matchAll(/\\(([^)]+)\\)/g);\n    for (const p of parts) tags.push(p[1]);\n    return { version: m[1], extra: tags };\n  },\n  // openjdk version \"21\" 2023-09-19 LTS\n  java: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/version\\s+\"([^\"]+)\"(?:\\s+(.+))?/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: m[2] ? [m[2].trim()] : [] };\n  },\n  // gcc (Ubuntu 13.3.0-6ubuntu2~24.04.1) 13.3.0 — use full distro version\n  gcc: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^gcc\\s+(?:\\((\\S+)\\s+([\\d.]+\\S*)\\)\\s+)?([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    // If distro info present, use full distro version (e.g. 13.3.0-6ubuntu2~24.04.1)\n    if (m[1] && m[2]) return { version: m[2], extra: [] };\n    return { version: m[3], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // g++ (Ubuntu 13.3.0-6ubuntu2~24.04.1) 13.3.0 — use full distro version\n  gpp: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^g\\+\\+\\s+(?:\\((\\S+)\\s+([\\d.]+\\S*)\\)\\s+)?([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    // If distro info present, use full distro version (e.g. 13.3.0-6ubuntu2~24.04.1)\n    if (m[1] && m[2]) return { version: m[2], extra: [] };\n    return { version: m[3], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // clang version 17.0.0 (https://github.com/... commit)\n  clang: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^clang\\s+version\\s+([\\d.]+(?:-\\S+)?)\\s*(?:\\(([^)]+)\\))?/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: m[2] ? [m[2].trim()] : [] };\n  },\n  // LLD 17.0.0 (compatible with GNU linkers) — only version number matters\n  lld: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^LLD\\s+([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // Python 3.14.3\n  python: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^Python\\s+([\\d.]+(?:\\S*)?)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // ruby 3.4.9 (2026-03-11 revision 76cca827ab) +PRISM [x86_64-linux]\n  ruby: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^ruby\\s+([\\d.]+(?:p\\d+)?)\\s*(?:\\(([^)]+)\\))?\\s*(.*)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    const extra = [];\n    if (m[2]) extra.push(m[2].trim());\n    const tail = m[3] ? m[3].trim() : '';\n    if (tail) extra.push(tail);\n    return { version: m[1], extra };\n  },\n  // Kotlin version 2.3.20-release-208 (JRE 21+35-LTS)\n  kotlin: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^Kotlin\\s+version\\s+([\\d.\\-\\w]+)\\s*(?:\\(([^)]+)\\))?/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: m[2] ? // Xvfb returns \"Unrecognized option: -version\" — this is handled by fixing the command\n  // to use dpkg fallback first\n\n  // agent 1.0.0 or similar\n  agent: raw => {\n    const m = per-tool parser, returning uniform format:\n *   \"<version>\" or \"<version> (<extra1>, <extra2>, ...)\"\n * Falls back to the raw string if no parser matches.\n * @param {string} key - Tool key (must match a key in VERSION_PARSERS)\n * @param {string} raw - Raw version string from command output\n * @returns {string} Parsed version string in uniform format\n */\nexport function parseVersion(key, raw) {\n  if (!raw) return raw;\n  const parser = VERSION_PARSERS[key];\n  if (!parser) return raw;\n  const result = parser(raw);\n  if (!result) return raw;\n  const { version, extra } = result;\n  if (extra && extra.length > 0) {\n    return `${version} (${extra.join(', ')})`;\n  }\n  return version;\n}\n\n/**\n * Command definitions for version checking\n * Each entry has: key, command, and optional fallbacks\n * @type {Array<{key: string, command: string, fallbacks?: string[]}>}\n */\nconst VERSION_COMMANDS = [\n  // AI Agents and Tools (--tool options)\n  { key: {boolean} verbose - Enable verbose logging\n * @returns {Promise<{key: string, value: string|null}>}\n */\nasync function executeVersionCommand(cmdDef, verbose) {\n  let result = await execCommandAsync(cmdDef.command);\n\n  // Try fallbacks if primary command failed\n  if (!result && cmdDef.fallbacks) {\n    for (const fallback of cmdDef.fallbacks) {\n      result = await execCommandAsync(fallback);\n      if (result) break;\n    }\n  }\n\n  if (verbose && result) {\n    console.log(`[VERBOSE] ${cmdDef.key}: ${result}`);\n  } else if (verbose && !result) {\n           " const execAsync = promisify(exec);",
          " ",
          " /**",
          "+ * Month name/abbreviation to zero-padded number mapping",
          "+ */",
          "+const MONTH_MAP = {",
          "+  jan: '01', january: '01',",
          "+  feb: '02', february: '02',",
          "+  mar: '03', march: '03',",
          "+  apr: '04', april: '04',",
          "+  may: '05',",
          "+  jun: '06', june: '06',",
          "+  jul: '07', july: '07',",
          "+  aug: '08', august: '08',",
          "+  sep: '09', september: '09',",
          "+  oct: '10', october: '10',",
          "+  nov: '11', november: '11',",
          "+  dec: '12', december: '12',",
          "+};",
          "+",
          "+/**",
          "+ * Normalize a date string to ISO format (YYYY-MM-DD).",
          "+ * Handles formats like:",
          "+ *   \"20-Aug-23\"       → \"2023-08-20\"",
          "+ *   \"20 April 2009\"   → \"2009-04-20\"",
          "+ *   \"July 5th 2008\"   → \"2008-07-05\"",
          "+ *   \"Jan 13 2026\"     → \"2026-01-13\"",
          "+ *   \"2024-02-29\"      → \"2024-02-29\" (passthrough)",
          "+ * Returns the original string if parsing fails.",
          "+ * @param {string} dateStr - Date string to normalize",
          "+ * @returns {string} ISO date string or original",
          "+ */",
          "+export function normalizeDate(dateStr) {",
          "+  if (!dateStr) return dateStr;",
          "+  const s = dateStr.trim();",
          "+",
          "+  // Already ISO: YYYY-MM-DD",
          "+  if (/^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}$/.test(s)) return s;",
          "+",
          "+  // \"DD-Mon-YY\" (e.g. \"20-Aug-23\")",
          "+  const dmy = s.match(/^(\\d{1,2})-([A-Za-z]{3})-(\\d{2})$/);",
          "+  if (dmy) {",
          "+    const month = MONTH_MAP[dmy[2].toLowerCase()];",
          "+    if (month) {",
          "+      const year = parseInt(dmy[3], 10);",
          "+      const fullYear = year >= 70 ? `19${dmy[3]}` : `20${dmy[3]}`;",
          "+      return `${fullYear}-${month}-${dmy[1].padStart(2, '0')}`;",
          "+    }",
          "+  }",
          "+",
          "+  // \"DD Month YYYY\" (e.g. \"20 April 2009\")",
          "+  const dmY = s.match(/^(\\d{1,2})\\s+([A-Za-z]+)\\s+(\\d{4})$/);",
          "+  if (dmY) {",
          "+    const month = MONTH_MAP[dmY[2].toLowerCase()];",
          "+    if (month) return `${dmY[3]}-${month}-${dmY[1].padStart(2, '0')}`;",
          "+  }",
          "+",
          "+  // \"Month DDth YYYY\" or \"Month DD YYYY\" (e.g. \"July 5th 2008\", \"Jan 13 2026\")",
          "+  const mdY = s.match(/^([A-Za-z]+)\\s+(\\d{1,2})(?:st|nd|rd|th)?\\s+(\\d{4})$/);",
          "+  if (mdY) {",
          "+    const month = MONTH_MAP[mdY[1].toLowerCase()];",
          "+    if (month) return `${mdY[3]}-${month}-${mdY[2].padStart(2, '0')}`;",
          "+  }",
          "+",
          "+  // \"Month DD YYYY HH:MM:SS\" (e.g. \"Jan 13 2026 22:36:55\")",
          "+  const mdYt = s.match(/^([A-Za-z]+)\\s+(\\d{1,2})\\s+(\\d{4})\\s+(\\d{2}:\\d{2}:\\d{2})$/);",
          "+  if (mdYt) {",
          "+    const month = MONTH_MAP[mdYt[1].toLowerCase()];",
          "+    if (month) return `${mdYt[3]}-${month}-${mdYt[2].padStart(2, '0')} ${mdYt[4]}`;",
          "+  }",
          "+",
          "+  return s;",
          "+}",
          "+",
          "+/**",
          "+ * Strip OS/architecture info from a version extra string.",
          "+ * Removes patterns like \"x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu\", \"linux/amd64\", \"[x86_64-linux]\", etc.",
          "+ * @param {string} s - String to clean",
          "+ * @returns {string} Cleaned string",
          "+ */",
          "+function stripArchInfo(s) {",
          "+  if (!s) return s;",
          "+  return s",
          "+    // Remove bracketed arch like [x86_64-linux]",
          "+    .replace(/\\[[\\w-]+\\]\\s*/g, '')",
          "+    // Remove triple-target patterns like x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu, x86_64-pc-linux-gnu",
          "+    .replace(/\\b\\w+[-_]\\w+[-_]\\w+[-_]\\w+\\b/g, '')",
          "+    // Remove platform/arch patterns like linux/amd64",
          "+    .replace(/\\b(?:linux|darwin|win32|windows)\\/\\w+\\b/gi, '')",
          "+    .trim();",
          "+}",
          "+",
          "+/**",
          "  * Execute a command asynchronously and return its output, or null if it fails",
          "  * @param {string} command - Command to execute",
          "  * @param {number} timeout - Timeout in milliseconds (default: 5000ms)"
        ]
      }

    "originalFile": "#!/usr/bin/env node\n\n// Version information library for hive-mind project\n// Provides comprehensive version information for bot, commands, and runtime\n//\n// Performance command).\n\nimport { getVersion } from './version.lib.mjs';\nimport { exec } from 'child_process';\nimport { promisify } from 'util';\n\nconst execAsync = promisify(exec);\n\n/**\n * Month name/abbreviation to zero-padded number mapping\n */\nconst MONTH_MAP = {\n  jan: '01', january: '01',\n  feb: '02', february: '02',\n  mar: '03', march: '03',\n  apr: '04', april: '04',\n  may: '05',\n  jun: '06', june: '06',\n  jul: '07', july: '07',\n  aug: '08', august: '08',\n  sep: '09', september: '09',\n  oct: '10', october: '10',\n  nov: '11', november: '11',\n  dec: '12', december: '12',\n};\n\n/**\n * Normalize a date string to ISO format (YYYY-MM-DD).\n * Handles formats like:\n *   \"20-Aug-23\"       → \"2023-08-20\"\n *   \"20 April 2009\"   → \"2009-04-20\"\n *   \"July 5th 2008\"   → \"2008-07-05\"\n *   \"Jan 13 2026\"     → \"2026-01-13\"\n *   \"2024-02-29\"      → \"2024-02-29\" (passthrough)\n * Returns the original string if parsing fails.\n * @param {string} dateStr - Date string to normalize\n * @returns {string} ISO date string or original\n */\nexport function normalizeDate(dateStr) {\n  if (!dateStr) return dateStr;\n  const s = dateStr.trim();\n\n  // Already ISO: YYYY-MM-DD\n  if (/^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}$/.test(s)) return s;\n\n  // \"DD-Mon-YY\" (e.g. \"20-Aug-23\")\n  const dmy = s.match(/^(\\d{1,2})-([A-Za-z]{3})-(\\d{2})$/);\n  if (dmy) {\n    const month = MONTH_MAP[dmy[2].toLowerCase()];\n    if (month) {\n      const year = parseInt(dmy[3], 10);\n      const fullYear = year >= 70 ? `19${dmy[3]}` : `20${dmy[3]}`;\n      return `${fullYear}-${month}-${dmy[1].padStart(2, '0')}`;\n    }\n  }\n\n  // \"DD Month YYYY\" (e.g. \"20 April 2009\")\n  const dmY = s.match(/^(\\d{1,2})\\s+([A-Za-z]+)\\s+(\\d{4})$/);\n  if (dmY) {\n    const month = MONTH_MAP[dmY[2].toLowerCase()];\n    if (month) return `${dmY[3]}-${month}-${dmY[1].padStart(2, '0')}`;\n  }\n\n  // \"Month DDth YYYY\" or \"Month DD YYYY\" (e.g. \"July 5th 2008\", \"Jan 13 2026\")\n  const mdY = s.match(/^([A-Za-z]+)\\s+(\\d{1,2})(?:st|nd|rd|th)?\\s+(\\d{4})$/);\n  if (mdY) {\n    const month = MONTH_MAP[mdY[1].toLowerCase()];\n    if (month) return `${mdY[3]}-${month}-${mdY[2].padStart(2, '0')}`;\n  }\n\n  // \"Month DD YYYY HH:MM:SS\" (e.g. \"Jan 13 2026 22:36:55\")\n  const mdYt = s.match(/^([A-Za-z]+)\\s+(\\d{1,2})\\s+(\\d{4})\\s+(\\d{2}:\\d{2}:\\d{2})$/);\n  if (mdYt) {\n    const month = MONTH_MAP[mdYt[1].toLowerCase()];\n    if (month) return `${mdYt[3]}-${month}-${mdYt[2].padStart(2, '0')} ${mdYt[4]}`;\n  }\n\n  return s;\n}\n\n/**\n * Strip OS/architecture info from a version extra string.\n * Removes patterns like \"x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu\", \"linux/amd64\", \"[x86_64-linux]\", etc.\n * @param {string} s - String to clean\n * @returns {string} Cleaned string\n */\nfunction stripArchInfo(s) {\n  if (!s) return s;\n  return s\n    // Remove bracketed arch like [x86_64-linux]\n    .replace(/\\[[\\w-]+\\]\\s*/g, '')\n    // Remove triple-target patterns like x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu, x86_64-pc-linux-gnu\n    .replace(/\\b\\w+[-_]\\w+[-_]\\w+[-_]\\w+\\b/g, '')\n    // Remove platform/arch patterns like linux/amd64\n    .replace(/\\b(?:linux|darwin|win32|windows)\\/\\w+\\b/gi, '')\n    .trim();\n}\n\n/**\n * Execute a command asynchronously and return its output, or null if it fails\n * @param {string} command - Command to execute\n * @param {number} timeout - Timeout in milliseconds (default: 5000ms)\n * @returns {Promise<string|null>} Command output or null\n */\nasync function execCommandAsync(command, timeout = 5000) {\n  try {\n    const { stdout } = await execAsync(command, { timeout, maxBuffer: 1024 * 1024 });\n    const trimmed = stdout.trim();\n    // Return null if the output looks like an error message\n    if (trimmed.includes('not found') || trimmed.includes('command not found') || trimmed === '') {\n      return null;\n    }\n    return trimmed;\n  } catch {\n    return null;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Per-tool regex parsers to normalize raw --version output into uniform format:\n *   <version> (<commit>, <revision>, <date>, etc.)\n *\n * Each parser returns { version, extra[] } or null if it doesn't match.\n * The `version` is the most specific version string (for bug reporting).\n * Items in `extra` are joined with \", \" and placed in parentheses.\n *\n * @type {Record<string, (raw: string) => {version: string, extra: string[]} | null>}\n */\nconst VERSION_PARSERS = {\n  // rustc 1.94.1 (e408947bf 2026-03-25)\n  rust: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^rustc\\s+([\\d.]+(?:-\\S+)?)\\s*(?:\\(([^)]+)\\))?/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    const extra = m[2] ? m[2].trim().split(/\\s+/) : [];\n    return { version: m[1], extra };\n  },\n  // cargo 1.94.1 (29ea6fb6a 2026-03-24)\n  cargo: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^cargo\\s+([\\d.]+(?:-\\S+)?)\\s*(?:\\(([^)]+)\\))?/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    const extra = m[2] ? m[2].trim().split(/\\s+/) : [];\n    return { version: m[1], extra };\n  },\n  // go version go1.26.1 linux/amd64\n  go: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/go([\\d.]+(?:\\S*)?)\\s+(.*)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [m[2].trim()] };\n  },\n  // PHP 8.3.30 (cli) (built: Jan 13 2026 22:36:55) (NTS)\n  php: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^PHP\\s+([\\d.]+(?:-\\S+)?)\\s*(.*)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    const tags = [];\n    const parts = m[2].matchAll(/\\(([^)]+)\\)/g);\n    for (const p of parts) tags.push(p[1]);\n    return { version: m[1], extra: tags };\n  },\n  // openjdk version \"21\" 2023-09-19 LTS\n  java: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/version\\s+\"([^\"]+)\"(?:\\s+(.+))?/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: m[2] ? [m[2].trim()] : [] };\n  },\n  // gcc (Ubuntu 13.3.0-6ubuntu2~24.04.1) 13.3.0 — use full distro version\n  gcc: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^gcc\\s+(?:\\((\\S+)\\s+([\\d.]+\\S*)\\)\\s+)?([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    // If distro info present, use full distro version (e.g. 13.3.0-6ubuntu2~24.04.1)\n    if (m[1] && m[2]) return { version: m[2], extra: [] };\n    return { version: m[3], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // g++ (Ubuntu 13.3.0-6ubuntu2~24.04.1) 13.3.0 — use full distro version\n  gpp: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^g\\+\\+\\s+(?:\\((\\S+)\\s+([\\d.]+\\S*)\\)\\s+)?([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    // If distro info present, use full distro version (e.g. 13.3.0-6ubuntu2~24.04.1)\n    if (m[1] && m[2]) return { version: m[2], extra: [] };\n    return { version: m[3], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // clang version 17.0.0 (https://github.com/... commit)\n  clang: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^clang\\s+version\\s+([\\d.]+(?:-\\S+)?)\\s*(?:\\(([^)]+)\\))?/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: m[2] ? [m[2].trim()] : [] };\n  },\n  // LLD 17.0.0 (compatible with GNU linkers) — only version number matters\n  lld: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^LLD\\s+([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // Python 3.14.3\n  python: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^Python\\s+([\\d.]+(?:\\S*)?)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // ruby 3.4.9 (2026-03-11 revision 76cca827ab) +PRISM [x86_64-linux]\n  ruby: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^ruby\\s+([\\d.]+(?:p\\d+)?)\\s*(?:\\(([^)]+)\\))?\\s*(.*)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    const extra = [];\n    if (m[2]) extra.push(m[2].trim());\n    const tail = m[3] ? m[3].trim() : '';\n    if (tail) extra.push(tail);\n    return { version: m[1], extra };\n  },\n  // Kotlin version 2.3.20-release-208 (JRE 21+35-LTS)\n  kotlin: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^Kotlin\\s+version\\s+([\\d.\\-\\w]+)\\s*(?:\\(([^)]+)\\))?/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: m[2] ? [m[2].trim()] : [] };\n  },\n  // Swift version 6.0.3 (swift-6.0.3-RELEASE)\n  swift: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^Swift\\s+version\\s+([\\d.]+(?:\\.\\d+)?)\\s*(?:\\(([^)]+)\\))?/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: m[2] ? [m[2].trim()] : [] };\n  },\n  // R version 4.3.3 (2024-02-29) -- \"Angel Food Cake\"\n  r: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^R\\s+version\\s+([\\d.]+)\\s*(?:\\(([^)]+)\\))?(?:\\s+--\\s+\"([^\"]+)\")?/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    const extra = [];\n    if (m[2]) extra.push(m[2]);\n    if (m[3]) extra.push(m[3]);\n    return { version: m[1], extra };\n  },\n  // git version 2.43.0\n  git: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^git\\s+version\\s+([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // gh version 2.89.0 (2026-03-26)\n  gh: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^gh\\s+version\\s+([\\d.]+)\\s*(?:\\(([^)]+)\\))?/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: m[2] ? m[2]\n          .split(',')\n          .map(s => s.trim().replace(/\\)$/, ''))\n          .filter(Boolean)\n      : [];\n    return { version: m[1], extra };\n  },\n  // Google Chrome 146.0.7680.164\n  chrome: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^Google\\s+Chrome\\s+([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // Chromium 137.0.7151.0\n  chromium: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^Chromium\\s+([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // Mozilla Firefox 139.0\n  firefox: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^Mozilla\\s+Firefox\\s+([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // Microsoft Edge 146.0.3856.84\n  msedge: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^Microsoft\\s+Edge\\s+([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // deno 2.7.9 (stable, release, x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu)\n  deno: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^deno\\s+([\\d.]+)\\s*(?:\\(([^)]+)\\))?/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    const extra = m[2]\n      ? [m[2]] : [] };\n  },\n  // GitHub Copilot CLI 1.0.14.\\nRun 'copilot update'...\n  copilot: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    // Strip trailing dot from version (e.g. \"1.0.14.\" -> \"1.0.14\")\n    const version = m[1].replace(/\\.$/, '');\n    return { version, extra: [] };\n  },\n  // pyenv 2.6.26\n  pyenv: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^pyenv\\s+([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // /workspace/.perl5/bin/perlbrew  - App::perlbrew/1.02\n  perlbrew: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/App::perlbrew\\/([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // rbenv 1.3.2-20-g23c3041\n  rbenv: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^rbenv\\s+([\\d.]+(?:-[\\w]+)*)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // Homebrew 5.1.2\n  brew: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^Homebrew\\s+([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // This is Zip 3.0 (July 5th 2008), by Info-ZIP.\n  zip: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/Zip\\s+([\\d.]+)\\s*(?:\\(([^)]+)\\))?/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: m[2] ? [m[2]] : [] };\n  },\n  // UnZip 6.00 of 20 April 2009, by Debian.\n  unzip: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/UnZip\\s+([\\d.]+)\\s*(?:of\\s+([^,]+))?/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: m[2] ? [m[2].trim()] : [] };\n  },\n  // ii  xvfb  2:21.1.12-1ubuntu1.5  amd64  Virtual Framebuffer...\n  xvfb: raw => {\n    // dpkg output format\n    const dpkg = raw.match(/^ii\\s+xvfb\\s+(\\S+)/);\n    if (dpkg) {\n      // Strip epoch (e.g. \"2:21.1.12-1ubuntu1.5\" -> \"21.1.12-1ubuntu1.5\")\n      const ver = dpkg[1].replace(/^\\d+:/, '');\n      return { version: ver, extra: [] };\n    }\n    // X.Org X Server version output (if it ever works)\n    const xorg = raw.match(/X\\.Org\\s+X\\s+Server\\s+([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (xorg) return { version: xorg[1], extra: [] };\n    return null;\n  },\n  // Xvfb returns \"Unrecognized option: -version\" — this is handled by fixing the command\n  // to use dpkg fallback first\n\n  // agent 1.0.0 or similar\n  agent: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // codex-cli 0.117.0 or similar\n  codex: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // opencode 1.3.10 or similar\n  opencode: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // qwen-code version\n  qwenCode: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // gemini version\n  gemini: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n};\n\n/**\n * Parse a raw version string using the per-tool parser, returning uniform format:\n *   \"<version>\" or \"<version> (<extra1>, <extra2>, ...)\"\n * Falls back to the raw string if no parser matches.\n * @param {string} key - Tool key (must match a key in VERSION_PARSERS)\n * @param {string} raw - Raw version string from command output\n * @returns {string} Parsed version string in uniform format\n */\nexport function parseVersion(key, raw) {\n  if (!raw) return raw;\n  const parser = VERSION_PARSERS[key];\n  if (!parser) return raw;\n  const result = parser(raw);\n  if (!result) return raw;\n  const { version, extra } = result;\n  if (extra && extra.length > 0) {\n    return `${version} (${extra.join(', ')})`;\n  }\n  return version;\n}\n\n/**\n * Command definitions for version checking\n * Each entry has: key, command, and optional fallbacks\n * @type {Array<{key: string, command: string, fallbacks?: string[]}>}\n */\nconst VERSION_COMMANDS = [\n  // AI Agents and Tools (--tool options)\n  { key: 'claudeCode', command: 'claude --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'agent', command: 'agent --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'codex', command: 'codex --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'opencode', command: 'opencode --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'qwenCode', command: 'qwen-code --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'gemini', command: 'gemini --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'copilot', command: 'copilot --version 2>&1' },\n\n  // Browser Automation\n  { key: 'playwright', command: 'playwright --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'playwrightTest', command: \"npm list -g @playwright/test --depth=0 2>&1 | grep @playwright/test | awk '{print $2}'\" },\n  { key: 'playwrightMcp', command: \"npm list -g @playwright/mcp --depth=0 2>&1 | grep @playwright/mcp | awk '{print $2}'\" },\n  { key: 'playwrightMcpStatus', command: 'timeout 5 claude mcp list 2>&1 | grep -i playwright | head -1' },\n  { key: 'puppeteerBrowsers', command: \"npm list -g @puppeteer/browsers --depth=0 2>&1 | grep @puppeteer/browsers | awk '{print $2}'\" },\n\n  // Browsers (installed via Playwright)\n  { key: 'chrome', command: 'google-chrome --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'chromium', command: 'chromium --version 2>&1', fallbacks: ['chromium-browser --version 2>&1', \"ls ~/.cache/ms-playwright/ 2>/dev/null | grep -oE 'chromium-[0-9]+' | head -1\"] },\n  { key: 'firefox', command: 'firefox --version 2>&1', fallbacks: [\"ls ~/.cache/ms-playwright/ 2>/dev/null | grep -oE 'firefox-[0-9]+' | head -1\"] },\n  { key: 'msedge', command: 'microsoft-edge --version 2>&1', fallbacks: ['microsoft-edge-stable --version 2>&1'] },\n  { key: 'webkit', command: \"ls ~/.cache/ms-playwright/ 2>/dev/null | grep -oE 'webkit-[0-9]+' | head -1\" },\n\n  // JavaScript/Node.js ecosystem\n  { key: 'bun', command: 'bun --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'deno', command: 'deno --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'npm', command: 'npm --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'nvm', command: 'nvm --version 2>&1' },\n\n  // Python ecosystem\n  { key: 'python', command: 'python --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'pyenv', command: 'pyenv --version 2>&1' },\n\n  // Rust ecosystem\n  { key: 'rust', command: 'rustc --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'cargo', command: 'cargo --version 2>&1' },\n\n  // Java ecosystem\n  { key: 'java', command: 'java -version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'sdkman', command: \"sdk version 2>&1 | grep -oE '[0-9]+\\\\.[0-9]+\\\\.[0-9]+'\" },\n\n  // Go\n  { key: 'go', command: 'go version 2>&1' },\n\n  // PHP\n  { key: 'php', command: 'php --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n\n  // .NET\n  { key: 'dotnet', command: 'dotnet --version 2>&1' },\n\n  // Perl ecosystem\n  { key: 'perl', command: \"perl -v 2>&1 | grep -oE 'v[0-9]+\\\\.[0-9]+\\\\.[0-9]+'\" },\n  { key: 'perlbrew', command: 'perlbrew --version 2>&1' },\n\n  // OCaml/Rocq ecosystem\n  { key: 'ocaml', command: 'ocaml --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'opam', command: 'opam --version 2>&1' },\n  // Rocq has fallback commands (rocq -> rocqc -> coqc)\n  { key: 'rocq', command: 'rocq -v 2>&1 | head -n1', fallbacks: ['rocqc --version 2>&1 | head -n1', 'coqc --version 2>&1 | head -n1'] },\n\n  // Lean ecosystem\n  { key: 'lean', command: 'lean --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'elan', command: 'elan --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'lake', command: 'lake --version 2>&1' },\n\n  // C/C++ Development Tools\n  { key: 'gcc', command: 'gcc --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'gpp', command: 'g++ --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'clang', command: 'clang --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'llvm', command: 'llvm-config --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'lld', command: 'ld.lld --version 2>&1 | head -n1', fallbacks: ['lld --version 2>&1 | head -n1'] },\n  { key: 'make', command: 'make --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'cmake', command: 'cmake --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n\n  // Ruby ecosystem\n  { key: 'ruby', command: 'ruby --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'rbenv', command: 'rbenv --version 2>&1' },\n\n  // Kotlin\n  { key: 'kotlin', command: 'kotlin -version 2>&1' },\n\n  // Swift\n  { key: 'swift', command: 'swift --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n\n  // R\n  { key: 'r', command: 'R --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n\n  // Development Tools\n  { key: 'git', command: 'git --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'gh', command: 'gh --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'glab', command: 'glab --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'brew', command: 'brew --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'nasm', command: 'nasm --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'fasm', command: 'fasm 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'curl', command: 'curl --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'wget', command: 'wget --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'zip', command: 'zip --version 2>&1 | head -n2 | tail -n1' },\n  { key: 'unzip', command: 'unzip -v 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'expect', command: 'expect -version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'screen', command: 'screen --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'xvfb', command: 'dpkg -l xvfb 2>/dev/null | grep \"^ii\" | head -1', fallbacks: ['Xvfb -version 2>&1 | head -n1'] },\n];\n\n/**\n * Execute a version command with optional fallbacks\n * @param {{key: string, command: string, fallbacks?: string[]}} cmdDef - Command definition\n * @param {boolean} verbose - Enable verbose logging\n * @returns {Promise<{key: string, value: string|null}>}\n */\nasync function executeVersionCommand(cmdDef, verbose) {\n  let result = await execCommandAsync(cmdDef.command);\n\n  // Try fallbacks if primary command failed\n  if (!result && cmdDef.fallbacks) {\n    for (const fallback of cmdDef.fallbacks) {\n      result = await execCommandAsync(fallback);\n      if (result) break;\n    }\n  }\n\n  if (verbose && result) {\n    console.log(`[VERBOSE] ${cmdDef.key}: ${result}`);\n  } else if (verbose && !result) {\n    console.log(`[VERBOSE] ${cmdDef.key}: not found`);\n  }\n\n  return { key: cmdDef.key, value: result };\n}\n\n/**\n * Get comprehensive version information for all components\n * Uses Promise.all for parallel execution (issue #1320)\n * @param {boolean} verbose - Enable verbose logging\n * @param {string} [processVersion] - Optional: version from the running process (for restart warning)\n * @returns {Promise<Object>} Version information object\n */\nexport async function getVersionInfo(verbose = false, processVersion = null) {\n  const startTime = Date.now();\n\n  try {\n    if (verbose) {\n      console.log('[VERBOSE] Gathering version information (parallel execution)...');\n    }\n\n    // Get hive-mind package version\n    const packageVersion = await getVersion();\n    if (verbose) {\n      console.log(`[VERBOSE] Package version: ${packageVersion}`);\n    }\n\n    // Execute all version commands in parallel\n    const results = await Promise.all(VERSION_COMMANDS.map(cmd => executeVersionCommand(cmd, verbose)));\n\n    // Convert results array to object\n    const versions = {};\n    for (const { key, value } of results) {\n      versions[key] = value;\n    }\n\n    // Add Node.js version (always available from process)\n    versions.node = process.version;\n    if (verbose) {\n      console.log(`[VERBOSE] Node.js version: ${versions.node}`);\n    }\n\n    // Platform information\n    const platform = process.platform;\n    const arch = process.arch;\n    versions.platform = `${platform} (${arch})`;\n    if (verbose) {\n      console.log(`[VERBOSE] Platform: ${versions.platform}`);\n    }\n\n    // Check if process version differs from installed version (restart warning)\n    const needsRestart = processVersion && processVersion !== packageVersion;\n\n    // Build version info object\n    const versionInfo = {\n      success: true,\n      versions: {\n        // Hive-mind package (single entry, not duplicated)\n        hiveMind: packageVersion,\n        processVersion: processVersion || packageVersion,\n        needsRestart,\n\n        // AI Agents (--tool options)\n        claudeCode: versions.claudeCode,\n        agent: versions.agent,\n        codex: versions.codex,\n        opencode: versions.opencode,\n        qwenCode: versions.qwenCode,\n        gemini: versions.gemini,\n        copilot: versions.copilot,\n\n        // Browser Automation\n        playwright: versions.playwright,\n        playwrightTest: versions.playwrightTest,\n        playwrightMcp: versions.playwrightMcp,\n        playwrightMcpStatus: versions.playwrightMcpStatus,\n        puppeteerBrowsers: versions.puppeteerBrowsers,\n\n        // Browsers\n        chrome: versions.chrome,\n        chromium: versions.chromium,\n        firefox: versions.firefox,\n        msedge: versions.msedge,\n        webkit: versions.webkit,\n\n        // JavaScript/Node.js\n        node: versions.node,\n        bun: versions.bun,\n        deno: versions.deno,\n        npm: versions.npm,\n        nvm: versions.nvm,\n\n        // Python\n        python: versions.python,\n        pyenv:     "originalFile": "#!/usr/bin/env node\n\n// Version information library for hive-mind project\n// Provides comprehensive version information for bot, commands, and runtime\n//\n// Performance optimization (issue #1320):\n// Uses Promise.all for parallel command execution instead of sequential execSync.\n// This reduces version gathering time from ~30-150s to ~5s (limited by slowest command).\n\nimport { getVersion } from './version.lib.mjs';\nimport { exec } from 'child_process';\nimport { promisify } from 'util';\n\nconst execAsync = promisify(exec);\n\n/**\n * Month name/abbreviation to zero-padded number mapping\n */\nconst MONTH_MAP = {\n  jan: '01', january: '01',\n  feb: '02', february: '02',\n  mar: '03', march: '03',\n  apr: '04', april: '04',\n  may: '05',\n  jun: '06', june: '06',\n  jul: '07', july: '07',\n  aug: '08', august: '08',\n  sep: '09', september: '09',\n  oct: '10', october: '10',\n  nov: '11', november: '11',\n  dec: '12', december: '12',\n};\n\n/**\n * Normalize a date string to ISO format (YYYY-MM-DD).\n * Handles formats like:\n *   \"20-Aug-23\"       → \"2023-08-20\"\n *   \"20 April 2009\"   → \"2009-04-20\"\n *   \"July 5th 2008\"   → \"2008-07-05\"\n *   \"Jan 13 2026\"     → \"2026-01-13\"\n *   \"2024-02-29\"      → \"2024-02-29\" (passthrough)\n * Returns the original string if parsing fails.\n * @param {string} dateStr - Date string to normalize\n * @returns {string} ISO date string or original\n */\nexport function normalizeDate(dateStr) {\n  if (!dateStr) return dateStr;\n  const s = dateStr.trim();\n\n  // Already ISO: YYYY-MM-DD\n  if (/^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}$/.test(s)) return s;\n\n  // \"DD-Mon-YY\" (e.g. \"20-Aug-23\")\n  const dmy = s.match(/^(\\d{1,2})-([A-Za-z]{3})-(\\d{2})$/);\n  if (dmy) {\n    const month = MONTH_MAP[dmy[2].toLowerCase()];\n    if (month) {\n      const year = parseInt(dmy[3], 10);\n      const fullYear = year >= 70 ? `19${dmy[3]}` : `20${dmy[3]}`;\n      return `${fullYear}-${month}-${dmy[1].padStart(2, '0')}`;\n    }\n  }\n\n  // \"DD Month YYYY\" (e.g. \"20 April 2009\")\n  const dmY = s.match(/^(\\d{1,2})\\s+([A-Za-z]+)\\s+(\\d{4})$/);\n  if (dmY) {\n    const month = MONTH_MAP[dmY[2].toLowerCase()];\n    if (month) return `${dmY[3]}-${month}-${dmY[1].padStart(2, '0')}`;\n  }\n\n  // \"Month DDth YYYY\" or \"Month DD YYYY\" (e.g. \"July 5th 2008\", \"Jan 13 2026\")\n  const mdY = s.match(/^([A-Za-z]+)\\s+(\\d{1,2})(?:st|nd|rd|th)?\\s+(\\d{4})$/);\n  if (mdY) {\n    const month = MONTH_MAP[mdY[1].toLowerCase()];\n    if (month) return `${mdY[3]}-${month}-${mdY[2].padStart(2, '0')}`;\n  }\n\n  // \"Month DD YYYY HH:MM:SS\" (e.g. \"Jan 13 2026 22:36:55\")\n  const mdYt = s.match(/^([A-Za-z]+)\\s+(\\d{1,2})\\s+(\\d{4})\\s+(\\d{2}:\\d{2}:\\d{2})$/);\n  if (mdYt) {\n    const month = MONTH_MAP[mdYt[1].toLowerCase()];\n    if (month) return `${mdYt[3]}-${month}-${mdYt[2].padStart(2, '0')} ${mdYt[4]}`;\n  }\n\n  return s;\n}\n\n/**\n * Strip OS/architecture info from a version extra string.\n * Removes patterns like \"x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu\", \"linux/amd64\", \"[x86_64-linux]\", etc.\n * @param {string} s - String to clean\n * @returns {string} Cleaned string\n */\nfunction stripArchInfo(s) {\n  if (!s) return s;\n  return s\n    // Remove bracketed arch like [x86_64-linux]\n    .replace(/\\[[\\w-]+\\]\\s*/g, '')\n    // Remove triple-target patterns like x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu, x86_64-pc-linux-gnu\n    .replace(/\\b\\w+[-_]\\w+[-_]\\w+[-_]\\w+\\b/g, '')\n    // Remove platform/arch patterns like linux/amd64\n    .replace(/\\b(?:linux|darwin|win32|windows)\\/\\w+\\b/gi, '')\n    .trim();\n}\n\n/**\n * Execute a command asynchronously and return its output, or null if it fails\n * @param {string} command - Command to execute\n * @param {number} timeout - Timeout in milliseconds (default: 5000ms)\n * @returns {Promise<string|null>} Command output or null\n */\nasync function execCommandAsync(command, timeout = 5000) {\n  try {\n    const { stdout } = await execAsync(command, { timeout, maxBuffer: 1024 * 1024 });\n    const trimmed = stdout.trim();\n    // Return null if the output looks like an error message\n    if (trimmed.includes('not found') || trimmed.includes('command not found') || trimmed === '') {\n      return null;\n    }\n    return trimmed;\n  } catch {\n    return null;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Per-tool regex parsers to normalize raw --version output into uniform format:\n *   <version> (<commit>, <revision>, <date>, etc.)\n *\n * Each parser returns { version, extra[] } or null if it doesn't match.\n * The `version` is the most specific version string (for bug reporting).\n * Items in `extra` are joined with \", \" and placed in parentheses.\n *\n * @type {Record<string, (raw: string) => {version: string, extra: string[]} | null>}\n */\nconst VERSION_PARSERS = {\n  // rustc 1.94.1 (e408947bf 2026-03-25)\n  rust: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^rustc\\s+([\\d.]+(?:-\\S+)?)\\s*(?:\\(([^)]+)\\))?/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    const extra = m[2] ? m[2].trim().split(/\\s+/) : [];\n    return { version: m[1], extra };\n  },\n  // cargo 1.94.1 (29ea6fb6a 2026-03-24)\n  cargo: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^cargo\\s+([\\d.]+(?:-\\S+)?)\\s*(?:\\(([^)]+)\\))?/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    const extra = m[2] ? m[2].trim().split(/\\s+/) : [];\n    return { version: m[1], extra };\n  },\n  // go version go1.26.1 linux/amd64\n  go: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/go([\\d.]+(?:\\S*)?)\\s+(.*)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [m[2].trim()] };\n  },\n  // PHP 8.3.30 (cli) (built: Jan 13 2026 22:36:55) (NTS)\n  php: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^PHP\\s+([\\d.]+(?:-\\S+)?)\\s*(.*)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    const tags = [];\n    const parts = m[2].matchAll(/\\(([^)]+)\\)/g);\n    for (const p of parts) tags.push(p[1]);\n    return { version: m[1], extra: tags };\n  },\n  // openjdk version \"21\" 2023-09-19 LTS\n  java: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/version\\s+\"([^\"]+)\"(?:\\s+(.+))?/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: m[2] ? [m[2].trim()] : [] };\n  },\n  // gcc (Ubuntu 13.3.0-6ubuntu2~24.04.1) 13.3.0 — use full distro version\n  gcc: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^gcc\\s+(?:\\((\\S+)\\s+([\\d.]+\\S*)\\)\\s+)?([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    // If distro info present, use full distro version (e.g. 13.3.0-6ubuntu2~24.04.1)\n    if (m[1] && m[2]) return { version: m[2], extra: [] };\n    return { version: m[3], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // g++ (Ubuntu 13.3.0-6ubuntu2~24.04.1) 13.3.0 — use full distro version\n  gpp: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^g\\+\\+\\s+(?:\\((\\S+)\\s+([\\d.]+\\S*)\\)\\s+)?([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    // If distro info present, use full distro version (e.g. 13.3.0-6ubuntu2~24.04.1)\n    if (m[1] && m[2]) return { version: m[2], extra: [] };\n    return { version: m[3], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // clang version 17.0.0 (https://github.com/... commit)\n  clang: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^clang\\s+version\\s+([\\d.]+(?:-\\S+)?)\\s*(?:\\(([^)]+)\\))?/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: m[2] ? [m[2].trim()] : [] };\n  },\n  // LLD 17.0.0 (compatible with GNU linkers) — only version number matters\n  lld: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^LLD\\s+([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // Python 3.14.3\n  python: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^Python\\s+([\\d.]+(?:\\S*)?)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // ruby 3.4.9 (2026-03-11 revision 76cca827ab) +PRISM [x86_64-linux]\n  ruby: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^ruby\\s+([\\d.]+(?:p\\d+)?)\\s*(?:\\(([^)]+)\\))?\\s*(.*)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    const extra = [];\n    if (m[2]) extra.push(m[2].trim());\n    const tail = m[3] ? m[3].trim() : '';\n    if (tail) extra.push(tail);\n    return { version: m[1], extra };\n  },\n  // Kotlin version 2.3.20-release-208 (JRE 21+35-LTS)\n  kotlin: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^Kotlin\\s+version\\s+([\\d.\\-\\w]+)\\s*(?:\\(([^)]+)\\))?/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: m[2] ? [m[2].trim()] : [] };\n  },\n  // R version 4.3.3 (2024-02-29) -- \"Angel Food Cake\"\n  r: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^R\\s+version\\s+([\\d.]+)\\s*(?:\\(([^)]+)\\))?(?:\\s+--\\s+\"([^\"]+)\")?/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    const extra = [];\n    if (m[2]) extra.push(m[2]);\n    if (m[3]) extra.push(m[3]);\n    return { version: m[1], extra };\n  },\n  // git version 2.43.0\n  git: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^git\\s+version\\s+([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // gh version 2.89.0 (2026-03-26)\n  gh: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^gh\\s+version\\s+([\\d.]+)\\s*(?:\\(([^)]+)\\))?/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: m[2] ?
