    "originalFile": "#!/usr/bin/env node\n\n// Version information library for hive-mind project\n// Provides comprehensive version information for bot, commands, and runtime\n//\n// Performance optimization (issue #1320):\n// Uses Promise.all for parallel command execution instead of sequential execSync.\n// This reduces version gathering time from ~30-150s to ~5s (limited by slowest command).\n\nimport { getVersion } from './version.lib.mjs';\nimport { exec } from 'child_process';\nimport { promisify } from 'util';\n\nconst execAsync = promisify(exec);\n\n/**\n * Month name/abbreviation to zero-padded number mapping\n */\nconst MONTH_MAP = {\n  jan: '01', january: '01',\n  feb: '02', february: '02',\n  mar: '03', march: '03',\n  apr: '04', april: '04',\n  may: '05',\n  jun: '06', june: '06',\n  jul: '07', july: '07',\n  aug: '08', august: '08',\n  sep: '09', september: '09',\n  oct: '10', october: '10',\n  nov: '11', november: '11',\n  dec: '12', december: '12',\n};\n\n/**\n * Normalize a date string to ISO format (YYYY-MM-DD).\n * Handles formats like:\n *   \"20-Aug-23\"       → \"2023-08-20\"\n *   \"20 April 2009\"   → \"2009-04-20\"\n *   \"July 5th 2008\"   → \"2008-07-05\"\n *   \"Jan 13 2026\"     → \"2026-01-13\"\n *   \"2024-02-29\"      → \"2024-02-29\" (passthrough)\n * Returns the original string if parsing fails.\n * @param {string} dateStr - Date string to normalize\n * @returns {string} ISO date string or original\n */\nexport function normalizeDate(dateStr) {\n  if (!dateStr) return dateStr;\n  const s = dateStr.trim();\n\n  // Already ISO: YYYY-MM-DD\n  if (/^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}$/.test(s)) return s;\n\n  // \"DD-Mon-YY\" (e.g. \"20-Aug-23\")\n  const dmy = s.match(/^(\\d{1,2})-([A-Za-z]{3})-(\\d{2})$/);\n  if (dmy) {\n    const month = MONTH_MAP[dmy[2].toLowerCase()];\n    if (month) {\n      const year = parseInt(dmy[3], 10);\n      const fullYear = year >= 70 ? `19${dmy[3]}` : `20${dmy[3]}`;\n      return `${fullYear}-${month}-${dmy[1].padStart(2, '0')}`;\n    }\n  }\n\n  // \"DD Month YYYY\" (e.g. \"20 April 2009\")\n  const dmY = s.match(/^(\\d{1,2})\\s+([A-Za-z]+)\\s+(\\d{4})$/);\n  if (dmY) {\n    const month = MONTH_MAP[dmY[2].toLowerCase()];\n    if (month) return `${dmY[3]}-${month}-${dmY[1].padStart(2, '0')}`;\n  }\n\n  // \"Month DDth YYYY\" or \"Month DD YYYY\" (e.g. \"July 5th 2008\", \"Jan 13 2026\")\n  const mdY = s.match(/^([A-Za-z]+)\\s+(\\d{1,2})(?:st|nd|rd|th)?\\s+(\\d{4})$/);\n  if (mdY) {\n    const month = MONTH_MAP[mdY[1].toLowerCase()];\n    if (month) return `${mdY[3]}-${month}-${mdY[2].padStart(2, '0')}`;\n  }\n\n  // \"Month DD YYYY HH:MM:SS\" (e.g. \"Jan 13 2026 22:36:55\")\n  const mdYt = s.match(/^([A-Za-z]+)\\s+(\\d{1,2})\\s+(\\d{4})\\s+(\\d{2}:\\d{2}:\\d{2})$/);\n  if (mdYt) {\n    const month = MONTH_MAP[mdYt[1].toLowerCase()];\n    if (month) return `${mdYt[3]}-${month}-${mdYt[2].padStart(2, '0')} ${mdYt[4]}`;\n  }\n\n  return s;\n}\n\n/**\n * Strip OS/architecture info from a version extra string.\n * Removes patterns like \"x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu\", \"linux/amd64\", \"[x86_64-linux]\", etc.\n * @param {string} s - String to clean\n * @returns {string} Cleaned string\n */\nfunction stripArchInfo(s) {\n  if (!s) return s;\n  return s\n    // Remove bracketed arch like [x86_64-linux]\n    .replace(/\\[[\\w-]+\\]\\s*/g, '')\n    // Remove triple-target patterns like x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu, x86_64-pc-linux-gnu\n    .replace(/\\b\\w+[-_]\\w+[-_]\\w+[-_]\\w+\\b/g, '')\n    // Remove platform/arch patterns like linux/amd64\n    .replace(/\\b(?:linux|darwin|win32|windows)\\/\\w+\\b/gi, '')\n    .trim();\n}\n\n/**\n * Execute a command asynchronously and return its output, or null if it fails\n * @param {string} command - Command to execute\n * @param {number} timeout - Timeout in milliseconds (default: 5000ms)\n * @returns {Promise<string|null>} Command output or null\n */\nasync function execCommandAsync(command, timeout = 5000) {\n  try {\n    const { stdout } = await execAsync(command, { timeout, maxBuffer: 1024 * 1024 });\n    const trimmed = stdout.trim();\n    // Return null if the output looks like an error message\n    if (trimmed.includes('not found') || trimmed.includes('command not found') || trimmed === '') {\n      return null;\n    }\n    return trimmed;\n  } catch {\n    return null;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Per-tool regex parsers to normalize raw --version output into uniform format:\n *   <version> (<commit>, <revision>, <date>, etc.)\n *\n * Each parser returns { version, extra[] } or null if it doesn't match.\n * The `version` is the most specific version string (for bug reporting).\n * Items in `extra` are joined with \", \" and placed in parentheses.\n *\n * @type {Record<string, (raw: string) => {version: string, extra: string[]} | null>}\n */\nconst VERSION_PARSERS = {\n  // rustc 1.94.1 (e408947bf 2026-03-25)\n  rust: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^rustc\\s+([\\d.]+(?:-\\S+)?)\\s*(?:\\(([^)]+)\\))?/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    const extra = m[2] ? m[2].trim().split(/\\s+/) : [];\n    return { version: m[1], extra };\n  },\n  // cargo 1.94.1 (29ea6fb6a 2026-03-24)\n  cargo: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^cargo\\s+([\\d.]+(?:-\\S+)?)\\s*(?:\\(([^)]+)\\))?/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    const extra = m[2] ? m[2].trim().split(/\\s+/) : [];\n    return { version: m[1], extra };\n  },\n  // go version go1.26.1 linux/amd64 → strip platform/arch\n  go: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/go([\\d.]+(?:\\S*)?)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // PHP 8.3.30 (cli) (built: Jan 13 2026 22:36:55) (NTS) → strip cli, normalize date\n  php: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^PHP\\s+([\\d.]+(?:-\\S+)?)\\s*(.*)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    const tags = [];\n    const parts = m[2].matchAll(/\\(([^)]+)\\)/g);\n    for (const p of parts) {\n      const tag = p[1].trim();\n      // Skip \"cli\" — not meaningful for version display\n      if (tag === 'cli') continue;\n      // Normalize \"built: Jan 13 2026 22:36:55\" → \"2026-01-13 22:36:55\"\n      const built = tag.match(/^built:\\s+(.+)$/);\n      if (built) {\n        tags.push(normalizeDate(built[1]));\n        continue;\n      }\n      tags.push(tag);\n    }\n    return { version: m[1], extra: tags };\n  },\n  // openjdk version \"21\" 2023-09-19 LTS\n  java: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/version\\s+\"([^\"]+)\"(?:\\s+(.+))?/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: m[2] ? [m[2].trim()] : [] };\n  },\n  // gcc (Ubuntu 13.3.0-6ubuntu2~24.04.1) 13.3.0 → use base version only\n  gcc: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^gcc\\s+(?:\\([^)]*\\)\\s+)?([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // g++ (Ubuntu 13.3.0-6ubuntu2~24.04.1) 13.3.0 → use base version only\n  gpp: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^g\\+\\+\\s+(?:\\([^)]*\\)\\s+)?([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // clang version 17.0.0 (https://github.com/... 2e6139970eda) → strip URL, keep commit\n  clang: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^clang\\s+version\\s+([\\d.]+(?:-\\S+)?)\\s*(?:\\(([^)]+)\\))?/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    if (m[2]) {\n      // Remove URLs, keep only hex commit hashes\n      const parts = m[2].trim().split(/\\s+/).filter(p => !p.includes('://') && !p.includes('.git'));\n      const commitParts = parts.filter(p => /^[0-9a-f]{7,}$/i.test(p));\n      return { version: m[1], extra: commitParts };\n    }\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // LLD 17.0.0 (compatible with GNU linkers) — only version number matters\n  lld: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^LLD\\s+([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // Python 3.14.3\n  python: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^Python\\s+([\\d.]+(?:\\S*)?)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // ruby 3.4.9 (2026-03-11 revision 76cca827ab) +PRISM [x86_64-linux] → strip arch, reformat\n  ruby: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^ruby\\s+([\\d.]+(?:p\\d+)?)\\s*(?:\\(([^)]+)\\))?\\s*(.*)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    const extra = [];\n    if (m[2]) {\n      // Parse \"2026-03-11 revision 76cca827ab\" → commit, date\n      const revMatch = m[2].match(/^(\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2})\\s+revision\\s+(\\w+)$/);\n      if (revMatch) {\n        extra.push(revMatch[2]); // commit first\n        extra.push(revMatch[1]); // then date\n      } else {\n        extra.push(m[2].trim());\n      }\n    }\n    // Capture +PRISM or similar flags, but strip [arch] info\n    const tail = m[3] ? m[3].trim() : '';\n    if (tail) {\n      const cleaned = tail.replace(/\\[[\\w-]+\\]/g, '').trim();\n      if (cleaned) extra.push(cleaned);\n    }\n    return { version: m[1], extra };\n  },\n  // Kotlin version 2.3.20-release-208 (JRE 21+35-LTS) → strip -release-NNN suffix\n  kotlin: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^Kotlin\\s+version\\s+([\\d.]+)(?:-release-\\d+)?\\s*(?:\\(([^)]+)\\))?/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: m[2] ? [m[2].trim()] : [] };\n  },\n  // Swift version 6.0.3 (swift-6.0.3-RELEASE) → strip redundant release tag\n  swift: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^Swift\\s+version\\s+([\\d.]+(?:\\.\\d+)?)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // R version 4.3.3 (2024-02-29) -- \"Angel Food Cake\"\n  r: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^R\\s+version\\s+([\\d.]+)\\s*(?:\\(([^)]+)\\))?(?:\\s+--\\s+\"([^\"]+)\")?/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    const extra = [];\n    if (m[2]) extra.push(m[2]);\n    if (m[3]) extra.push(m[3]);\n    return { version: m[1], extra };\n  },\n  // git version 2.43.0\n  git: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^git\\s+version\\s+([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // gh version 2.89.0 (2026-03-26)\n  gh: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^gh\\s+version\\s+([\\d.]+)\\s*(?:\\(([^)]+)\\))?/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: m[2] ? [m[2]] : [] };\n  },\n  // glab version 1.36.0\n  glab: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^glab\\s+version\\s+([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // curl 8.19.0 (x86_64-pc-linux-gnu) libcurl/8.19.0 ... → strip arch info\n  curl: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^curl\\s+([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // GNU Wget 1.21.4 built on linux-gnu.\n  wget: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^GNU\\s+Wget\\s+([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // cmake version 3.28.3\n  cmake: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^cmake\\s+version\\s+([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // GNU {\n    const m = raw.match(/^NASM\\s+version\\s+([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // flat assembler  version 1.73.32\n  fasm: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/version\\s+([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // Screen version 4.09.01 (GNU) 20-Aug-23 → normalize date, strip GNU\n  screen: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^Screen\\s+version\\s+([\\d.]+)\\s*(?:\\([^)]*\\))?\\s*(.*)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    const extra = [];\n    const dateStr = m[2] ? m[2].trim() : '';\n    if (dateStr) extra.push(normalizeDate(dateStr));\n    return { version: m[1], extra };\n  },\n  // expect version 5.45.4\n  expect: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^expect\\s+version\\s+([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // The OCaml toplevel, version 5.4.1\n  ocaml: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/version\\s+([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // The Rocq Prover, version 9.1.1\n  rocq: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/version\\s+([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // elan 4.2.1 (3d5138e15 2026-03-18)\n  elan: raw => {\n    const m = (version 4.29.0, x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu, commit abc123, Release) → strip arch/Release\n  lean: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/version\\s+([\\d.]+)(?:,\\s*(.+?))\\)?$/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    const extra = m[2]\n      ? m[2]\n          .split(',')\n          .map(s => s.trim().replace(/\\)$/, ''))\n          .filter(s => {\n            if (!s) return false;\n            // Strip [];\n    return { version: m[1], extra };\n  },\n  // Google Chrome 146.0.7680.164\n  chrome: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^Google\\s+Chrome\\s+([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // Chromium 137.0.7151.0\n  chromium: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^Chromium\\s+([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // Mozilla Firefox 139.0\n  firefox: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^Mozilla\\s+Firefox\\s+([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // Microsoft Edge 146.0.3856.84\n  msedge: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^Microsoft\\s+Edge\\s+([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // deno 2.7.9 (stable, release, x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu) → keep only channel (stable)\n  deno: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^deno\\s+([\\d.]+)\\s*(?:\\(([^)]+)\\))?/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    const extra = m[2]\n      ? m[2]\n          .split(',')\n          .map(s => s.trim())\n          .filter(s => s && s !== 'release' && !s.includes('-') && !s.includes('/'))\n      : [];\n    return { version: m[1], extra };\n  },\n  // Version 1.58.2  (Playwright CLI)\n  playwright: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/(?:Version\\s+)?([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // @playwright/test@1.58.2\n  playwrightTest: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/@playwright\\/test@([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // @playwright/mcp@0.0.69 or `-- @playwright/mcp@0.0.69\n  playwrightMcp: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/@playwright\\/mcp@([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // @puppeteer/browsers@2.13.0\n  puppeteerBrowsers: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/@puppeteer\\/browsers@([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // 2.1.87 (Claude Code) → strip redundant product name\n  claudeCode: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // GitHub Copilot CLI 1.0.14.\\nRun 'copilot update'...\n  copilot: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    // Strip trailing dot from version (e.g. \"1.0.14.\" -> \"1.0.14\")\n    const version = m[1].replace(/\\.$/, '');\n    return { version, extra: [] };\n  },\n  // pyenv 2.6.26\n  pyenv: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^pyenv\\s+([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // /workspace/.perl5/bin/perlbrew  - App::perlbrew/1.02\n  perlbrew: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/App::perlbrew\\/([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // rbenv 1.3.2-20-g23c3041\n  rbenv: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^rbenv\\s+([\\d.]+(?:-[\\w]+)*)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // Homebrew 5.1.2\n  brew: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^Homebrew\\s+([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // This is Zip 3.0 (July 5th 2008), by Info-ZIP. → normalize date\n  zip: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/Zip\\s+([\\d.]+)\\s*(?:\\(([^)]+)\\))?/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: m[2] ? [normalizeDate(m[2])] : [] };\n  },\n  // UnZip 6.00 of 20 April 2009, by Debian. → normalize date\n  unzip: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/UnZip\\s+([\\d.]+)\\s*(?:of\\s+([^,]+))?/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: m[2] ? [normalizeDate(m[2].trim())] : [] };\n  },\n  // ii  xvfb  2:21.1.12-1ubuntu1.5  amd64  Virtual Framebuffer... → base version only\n  xvfb: raw => {\n    // dpkg output format\n    const dpkg = raw.match(/^ii\\s+xvfb\\s+(\\S+)/);\n    if (dpkg) {\n      // Strip epoch (e.g. \"2:21.1.12-1ubuntu1.5\" -> \"21.1.12-1ubuntu1.5\")\n      // Then strip distro suffix (e.g. \"21.1.12-1ubuntu1.5\" -> \"21.1.12\")\n      const ver = dpkg[1].replace(/^\\d+:/, '').replace(/-.*$/, '');\n      return { version: ver, extra: [] };\n    }\n    // X.Org X Server version output (if it ever works)\n    const xorg = raw.match(/X\\.Org\\s+X\\s+Server\\s+([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (xorg) return { version: xorg[1], extra: [] };\n    return null;\n  },\n  // Xvfb returns \"Unrecognized option: -version\" — this is handled by fixing the command\n  // to use dpkg fallback first\n\n  // agent 1.0.0 or similar\n  agent: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // codex-cli 0.117.0 or similar\n  codex: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // opencode 1.3.10 or similar\n  opencode: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // qwen-code version\n  qwenCode: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // gemini version\n  gemini: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n};\n\n/**\n * Parse a raw version string using the per-tool parser, returning uniform format:\n *   \"<version>\" or \"<version> (<extra1>, <extra2>, ...)\"\n * Falls back to the raw string if no parser matches.\n * @param {string} key - Tool key (must match a key in VERSION_PARSERS)\n * @param {string} raw - Raw version string from command output\n * @returns {string} Parsed version string in uniform format\n */\nexport function parseVersion(key, raw) {\n  if (!raw) return raw;\n  const parser = VERSION_PARSERS[key];\n  if (!parser) return raw;\n  const result = parser(raw);\n  if (!result) return raw;\n  const { version, extra } = result;\n  if (extra && extra.length > 0) {\n    return `${version} (${extra.join(', ')})`;\n  }\n  return version;\n}\n\n/**\n * Command definitions for version checking\n * Each entry has: key, command, and optional fallbacks\n * @type {Array<{key: string, command: string, fallbacks?: string[]}>}\n */\nconst VERSION_COMMANDS = [\n  // AI Agents and Tools (--tool options)\n  { key: 'claudeCode', command: 'claude --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'agent', command: 'agent --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'codex', command: 'codex --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'opencode', command: 'opencode --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'qwenCode', command: 'qwen-code --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'gemini', command: 'gemini --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'copilot', command: 'copilot --version 2>&1' },\n\n  // Browser Automation\n  { key: 'playwright', command: 'playwright --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'playwrightTest', command: \"npm list -g @playwright/test --depth=0 2>&1 | grep @playwright/test | awk '{print $2}'\" },\n  { key: 'playwrightMcp', command: \"npm list -g @playwright/mcp --depth=0 2>&1 | grep @playwright/mcp | awk '{print $2}'\" },\n  { key: 'playwrightMcpStatus', command: 'timeout 5 claude mcp list 2>&1 | grep -i playwright | head -1' },\n  { key: 'puppeteerBrowsers', command: \"npm list -g @puppeteer/browsers --depth=0 2>&1 | grep @puppeteer/browsers | awk '{print $2}'\" },\n\n  // Browsers (installed via Playwright)\n  { key: 'chrome', command: 'google-chrome --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'chromium', command: 'chromium --version 2>&1', fallbacks: ['chromium-browser --version 2>&1', \"ls ~/.cache/ms-playwright/ 2>/dev/null | grep -oE 'chromium-[0-9]+' | head -1\"] },\n  { key: 'firefox', command: 'firefox --version 2>&1', fallbacks: [\"ls ~/.cache/ms-playwright/ 2>/dev/null | grep -oE 'firefox-[0-9]+' | head -1\"] },\n  { key: 'msedge', command: 'microsoft-edge --version 2>&1', fallbacks: ['microsoft-edge-stable --version 2>&1'] },\n  { key: 'webkit', command: \"ls ~/.cache/ms-playwright/ 2>/dev/null | grep -oE 'webkit-[0-9]+' | head -1\" },\n\n  // JavaScript/Node.js ecosystem\n  { key: 'bun', command: 'bun --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'deno', command: 'deno --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'npm', command: 'npm --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'nvm', command: 'nvm --version 2>&1' },\n\n  // Python ecosystem\n  { key: 'python', command: 'python --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'pyenv', command: 'pyenv --version 2>&1' },\n\n  // Rust ecosystem\n  { key: 'rust', command: 'rustc --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'cargo', command: 'cargo --version 2>&1' },\n\n  // Java ecosystem\n  { key: 'java', command: 'java -version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'sdkman', command: \"sdk version 2>&1 | grep -oE '[0-9]+\\\\.[0-9]+\\\\.[0-9]+'\" },\n\n  // Go\n  { key: 'go', command: 'go version 2>&1' },\n\n  // PHP\n  { key: 'php', command: 'php --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n\n  // .NET\n  { key: 'dotnet', command: 'dotnet --version 2>&1' },\n\n  // Perl ecosystem\n  { key: 'perl', command: \"perl -v 2>&1 | grep -oE 'v[0-9]+\\\\.[0-9]+\\\\.[0-9]+'\" },\n  { key: 'perlbrew', command: 'perlbrew --version 2>&1' },\n\n  // OCaml/Rocq ecosystem\n  { key: 'ocaml', command: 'ocaml --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'opam', command: 'opam --version 2>&1' },\n  // Rocq has fallback commands (rocq -> rocqc -> coqc)\n  { key: 'rocq', command: 'rocq -v 2>&1 | head -n1', fallbacks: ['rocqc --version 2>&1 | head -n1', 'coqc --version 2>&1 | head -n1'] },\n\n  // Lean ecosystem\n  { key: 'lean', command: 'lean --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'elan', command: 'elan --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'lake', command: 'lake --version 2>&1' },\n\n  // C/C++ Development Tools\n  { key: 'gcc', command: 'gcc --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'gpp', command: 'g++ --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'clang', command: 'clang --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'llvm', command: 'llvm-config --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'lld', command: 'ld.lld --version 2>&1 | head -n1', fallbacks: ['lld --version 2>&1 | --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'rbenv', command: 'rbenv --version 2>&1' },\n\n  // Kotlin\n  { key: 'kotlin', command: 'kotlin -version 2>&1' },\n\n  // Swift\n  { key: 'swift', command: 'swift --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n\n  // R\n  { key: 'r', command: 'R --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n\n  // Development Tools\n  { key: 'git', command: 'git --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'gh', command: 'gh --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'glab', command: 'glab --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'brew', command: 'brew --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'nasm', command: 'nasm --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'fasm', command: 'fasm 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'curl', command: 'curl --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'wget', command: 'wget --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'zip', command: 'zip --version 2>&1 | head -n2 | tail -n1' },\n  { key: 'unzip', command: 'unzip -v 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'expect', command: 'expect -version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'screen', command: 'screen --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'xvfb', command: 'dpkg -l xvfb 2>/dev/null | grep \"^ii\" | head -1', fallbacks: ['Xvfb -version 2>&1 | head -n1'] },\n];\n\n/**\n * Execute a version command with optional fallbacks\n * @param {{key: string, command: string, fallbacks?: string[]}} cmdDef - Command definition\n * @param {boolean} verbose - Enable verbose logging\n * @returns {Promise<{key: string, value: string|null}>}\n */\nasync function executeVersionCommand(cmdDef, verbose) {\n  let result = await execCommandAsync(cmdDef.command);\n\n  // Try fallbacks if primary command failed\n  if (!result && cmdDef.fallbacks) {\n    for (const fallback of cmdDef.fallbacks) {\n      result = await execCommandAsync(fallback);\n      if (result) break;\n    }\n  }\n\n  if (verbose && result) {\n    console.log(`[VERBOSE] ${cmdDef.key}: ${result}`);\n  } else if (verbose && !result) {\n    console.log(`[VERBOSE] ${cmdDef.key}: not found`);\n  }\n\n  return { key: cmdDef.key, value: result };\n}\n\n/**\n * Get comprehensive version information for all components\n * Uses Promise.all for parallel execution (issue #1320)\n * @param {boolean} verbose - Enable verbose logging\n * @param {string} [processVersion] - Optional: version from the running process (for restart warning)\n * @returns {Promise<Object>} Version information object\n */\nexport async function getVersionInfo(verbose = false, processVersion = null) {\n  const startTime = Date.now();\n\n  try {\n    if (verbose) {\n      console.log('[VERBOSE] Gathering version information (parallel execution)...');\n    }\n\n    // Get hive-mind package version\n    const packageVersion = await getVersion();\n    if (verbose) {\n      console.log(`[VERBOSE] Package version: ${packageVersion}`);\n    }\n\n    // Execute all version commands in parallel\n    const results = await Promise.all(VERSION_COMMANDS.map(cmd => executeVersionCommand(cmd, verbose)));\n\n    // Convert results array to object\n    const versions = {};\n    for (const { key, value } of results) {\n      versions[key] = value;\n    }\n\n    // Add Node.js version (always available from process)\n    versions.node = process.version;\n    if (verbose) {\n      console.log(`[VERBOSE] Node.js version: ${versions.node}`);\n    }\n\n    // Platform information\n    const platform = process.platform;\n    const arch = process.arch;\n    versions.platform = `${platform} (${arch})`;\n    if (verbose) {\n      console.log(`[VERBOSE] Platform: ${versions.platform}`);\n    }\n\n    // Check if process version differs from installed version (restart warning)\n    const needsRestart = processVersion && processVersion !== packageVersion;\n\n    // Build version info object\n    const     "newString": "    lines.push('*🔨 C, C++, Assembly*');",
    "originalFile": "#!/usr/bin/env node\n\n// Version information library for hive-mind project\n// Provides comprehensive version information for bot, commands, and runtime\n//\n// Performance optimization (issue #1320):\n// Uses Promise.all for parallel command execution instead of sequential execSync.\n// This reduces version gathering time from ~30-150s to ~5s (limited by slowest command).\n\nimport { getVersion } from './version.lib.mjs';\nimport { exec } from 'child_process';\nimport { promisify } from 'util';\n\nconst execAsync = promisify(exec);\n\n/**\n * Month name/abbreviation to zero-padded number mapping\n */\nconst MONTH_MAP = {\n  jan: '01', january: '01',\n  feb: '02', february: '02',\n  mar: '03', march: '03',\n  apr: '04', april: '04',\n  may: '05',\n  jun: '06', june: '06',\n  jul: '07', july: '07',\n  aug: '08', august: '08',\n  sep: '09', september: '09',\n  oct: '10', october: '10',\n  nov: '11', november: '11',\n  dec: '12', december: '12',\n};\n\n/**\n * Normalize a date string to ISO format (YYYY-MM-DD).\n * Handles formats like:\n *   \"20-Aug-23\"       → \"2023-08-20\"\n *   \"20 April 2009\"   → \"2009-04-20\"\n *   \"July 5th 2008\"   → \"2008-07-05\"\n *   \"Jan 13 2026\"     → \"2026-01-13\"\n *   \"2024-02-29\"      → \"2024-02-29\" (passthrough)\n * Returns the original string if parsing fails.\n * @param {string} dateStr - Date string to normalize\n * @returns {string} ISO date string or original\n */\nexport function normalizeDate(dateStr) {\n  if (!dateStr) return dateStr;\n  const s = dateStr.trim();\n\n  // Already ISO: YYYY-MM-DD\n  if (/^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}$/.test(s)) return s;\n\n  // \"DD-Mon-YY\" (e.g. \"20-Aug-23\")\n  const dmy = s.match(/^(\\d{1,2})-([A-Za-z]{3})-(\\d{2})$/);\n  if (dmy) {\n    const month = MONTH_MAP[dmy[2].toLowerCase()];\n    if (month) {\n      const year = parseInt(dmy[3], 10);\n      const fullYear = year >= 70 ? `19${dmy[3]}` : `20${dmy[3]}`;\n      return `${fullYear}-${month}-${dmy[1].padStart(2, '0')}`;\n    }\n  }\n\n  // \"DD Month YYYY\" (e.g. \"20 April 2009\")\n  const dmY = s.match(/^(\\d{1,2})\\s+([A-Za-z]+)\\s+(\\d{4})$/);\n  if (dmY) {\n    const month = MONTH_MAP[dmY[2].toLowerCase()];\n    if (month) return `${dmY[3]}-${month}-${dmY[1].padStart(2, '0')}`;\n  }\n\n  // \"Month DDth YYYY\" or \"Month DD YYYY\" (e.g. \"July 5th 2008\", \"Jan 13 2026\")\n  const mdY = s.match(/^([A-Za-z]+)\\s+(\\d{1,2})(?:st|nd|rd|th)?\\s+(\\d{4})$/);\n  if (mdY) {\n    const month = MONTH_MAP[mdY[1].toLowerCase()];\n    if (month) return `${mdY[3]}-${month}-${mdY[2].padStart(2, '0')}`;\n  }\n\n  // \"Month DD YYYY HH:MM:SS\" (e.g. \"Jan 13 2026 22:36:55\")\n  const mdYt = s.match(/^([A-Za-z]+)\\s+(\\d{1,2})\\s+(\\d{4})\\s+(\\d{2}:\\d{2}:\\d{2})$/);\n  if (mdYt) {\n    const month = MONTH_MAP[mdYt[1].toLowerCase()];\n    if (month) return `${mdYt[3]}-${month}-${mdYt[2].padStart(2, '0')} ${mdYt[4]}`;\n  }\n\n  return s;\n}\n\n/**\n * Strip OS/architecture info from a version extra string.\n * Removes patterns like \"x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu\", \"linux/amd64\", \"[x86_64-linux]\", etc.\n * @param {string} s - String to clean\n * @returns {string} Cleaned string\n */\nfunction stripArchInfo(s) {\n  if (!s) return s;\n  return s\n    // Remove bracketed arch like [x86_64-linux]\n    .replace(/\\[[\\w-]+\\]\\s*/g, '')\n    // Remove triple-target patterns like x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu, x86_64-pc-linux-gnu\n    .replace(/\\b\\w+[-_]\\w+[-_]\\w+[-_]\\w+\\b/g, '')\n    // Remove platform/arch patterns like linux/amd64\n    .replace(/\\b(?:linux|darwin|win32|windows)\\/\\w+\\b/gi, '')\n    .trim();\n}\n\n/**\n * Execute a command asynchronously and return its output, or null if it fails\n * @param {string} command - Command to execute\n * @param {number} timeout - Timeout in milliseconds (default: 5000ms)\n * @returns {Promise<string|null>} Command output or null\n */\nasync function execCommandAsync(command, timeout = 5000) {\n  try {\n    const { stdout } = await execAsync(command, { timeout, maxBuffer: 1024 * 1024 });\n    const trimmed = stdout.trim();\n    // Return null if the output looks like an error message\n    if (trimmed.includes('not found') || trimmed.includes('command not found') || trimmed === '') {\n      return null;\n    }\n    return trimmed;\n  } catch {\n    return null;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Per-tool regex parsers to normalize raw --version output into uniform format:\n *   <version> (<commit>, <revision>, <date>, etc.)\n *\n * Each parser returns { version, extra[] } or null if it doesn't match.\n * The `version` is the most specific version string (for bug reporting).\n * Items in `extra` are joined with \", \" and placed in parentheses.\n *\n * @type {Record<string, (raw: string) => {version: string, extra: string[]} | null>}\n */\nconst VERSION_PARSERS = {\n  // rustc 1.94.1 (e408947bf 2026-03-25)\n  rust: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^rustc\\s+([\\d.]+(?:-\\S+)?)\\s*(?:\\(([^)]+)\\))?/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    const extra = m[2] ? m[2].trim().split(/\\s+/) : [];\n    return { version: m[1], extra };\n  },\n  // cargo 1.94.1 (29ea6fb6a 2026-03-24)\n  cargo: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^cargo\\s+([\\d.]+(?:-\\S+)?)\\s*(?:\\(([^)]+)\\))?/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    const extra = m[2] ? m[2].trim().split(/\\s+/) : [];\n    return { version: m[1], extra };\n  },\n  // go version go1.26.1 linux/amd64 → strip platform/arch\n  go: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/go([\\d.]+(?:\\S*)?)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // PHP 8.3.30 (cli) (built: Jan 13 2026 22:36:55) (NTS) → strip cli, normalize date\n  php: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^PHP\\s+([\\d.]+(?:-\\S+)?)\\s*(.*)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    const tags = [];\n    const parts = m[2].matchAll(/\\(([^)]+)\\)/g);\n    for (const p of parts) {\n      const tag = p[1].trim();\n      // Skip \"cli\" — not meaningful for version display\n      if (tag === 'cli') continue;\n      // Normalize \"built: Jan 13 2026 22:36:55\" → \"2026-01-13 22:36:55\"\n      const built = tag.match(/^built:\\s+(.+)$/);\n      if (built) {\n        tags.push(normalizeDate(built[1]));\n        continue;\n      }\n      tags.push(tag);\n    }\n    return { version: m[1], extra: tags };\n  },\n  // openjdk version \"21\" 2023-09-19 LTS\n  java: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/version\\s+\"([^\"]+)\"(?:\\s+(.+))?/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: m[2] ? [m[2].trim()] : [] };\n  },\n  // gcc (Ubuntu 13.3.0-6ubuntu2~24.04.1) 13.3.0 → use base version only\n  gcc: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^gcc\\s+(?:\\([^)]*\\)\\s+)?([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // g++ (Ubuntu 13.3.0-6ubuntu2~24.04.1) 13.3.0 → use base version only\n  gpp: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^g\\+\\+\\s+(?:\\([^)]*\\)\\s+)?([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // clang version 17.0.0 (https://github.com/... 2e6139970eda) → strip URL, keep commit\n  clang: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^clang\\s+version\\s+([\\d.]+(?:-\\S+)?)\\s*(?:\\(([^)]+)\\))?/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    if (m[2]) {\n      // Remove URLs, keep only hex commit hashes\n      const parts = m[2].trim().split(/\\s+/).filter(p => !p.includes('://') && !p.includes('.git'));\n      const commitParts = parts.filter(p => /^[0-9a-f]{7,}$/i.test(p));\n      return { version: m[1], extra: commitParts };\n    }\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // LLD 17.0.0 (compatible with GNU linkers) — only version number matters\n  lld: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^LLD\\s+([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // Python 3.14.3\n  python: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^Python\\s+([\\d.]+(?:\\S*)?)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // ruby 3.4.9 (2026-03-11 revision 76cca827ab) +PRISM [x86_64-linux] → strip arch, reformat\n  ruby: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^ruby\\s+([\\d.]+(?:p\\d+)?)\\s*(?:\\(([^)]+)\\))?\\s*(.*)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    const extra = [];\n    if (m[2]) {\n      // Parse \"2026-03-11 revision 76cca827ab\" → commit, date\n      const revMatch = m[2].match(/^(\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2})\\s+revision\\s+(\\w+)$/);\n      if (revMatch) {\n        extra.push(revMatch[2]); // commit first\n        extra.push(revMatch[1]); // then date\n      } else {\n        extra.push(m[2].trim());\n      }\n    }\n    // Capture +PRISM or similar flags, but strip [arch] info\n    const tail = m[3] ? m[3].trim() : '';\n    if (tail) {\n      const cleaned = tail.replace(/\\[[\\w-]+\\]/g, '').trim();\n      if (cleaned) extra.push(cleaned);\n    }\n    return { version: m[1], extra };\n  },\n  // Kotlin version 2.3.20-release-208 (JRE 21+35-LTS) → strip -release-NNN suffix\n  kotlin: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^Kotlin\\s+version\\s+([\\d.]+)(?:-release-\\d+)?\\s*(?:\\(([^)]+)\\))?/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: m[2] ? [m[2].trim()] : [] };\n  },\n  // Swift version 6.0.3 (swift-6.0.3-RELEASE) → strip redundant release tag\n  swift: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^Swift\\s+version\\s+([\\d.]+(?:\\.\\d+)?)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // R version 4.3.3 (2024-02-29) -- \"Angel Food Cake\" → normalize date\n  r: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^R\\s+version\\s+([\\d.]+)\\s*(?:\\(([^)]+)\\))?(?:\\s+--\\s+\"([^\"]+)\")?/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    const extra = [];\n    if (m[2]) extra.push(normalizeDate(m[2]));\n    if (m[3]) extra.push(m[3]);\n    return { version: m[1], extra };\n  },\n  // git version 2.43.0\n  git: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^git\\s+version\\s+([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // gh version 2.89.0 (2026-03-26)\n  gh: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^gh\\s+version\\s+([\\d.]+)\\s*(?:\\(([^)]+)\\))?/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: m[2] ? // curl 8.19.0 (x86_64-pc-linux-gnu) libcurl/8.19.0 ... → strip arch info\n  curl: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^curl\\s+([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // GNU Wget 1.21.4 built on linux-gnu.\n  wget: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^GNU\\s+Wget\\s+([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] // Microsoft Edge 146.0.3856.84\n  msedge: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^Microsoft\\s+Edge\\s+([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // deno 2.7.9 (stable, release, x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu) → keep only channel (stable)\n  deno: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^deno\\s+([\\d.]+)\\s*(?:\\(([^)]+)\\))?/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    const extra = m[2]\n      ? m[2]\n          .split(',')\n          .map(s => s.trim())\n          .filter(s => s && s !== 'release' && !s.includes('-') && !s.includes('/'))\n      : [];\n    return { [normalizeDate(m[2].trim())] : [] };\n  },\n  // ii  xvfb  2:21.1.12-1ubuntu1.5  amd64  Virtual Framebuffer... → base version only\n  xvfb: raw => {\n    // dpkg output format\n    const dpkg = raw.match(/^ii\\s+xvfb\\s+(\\S+)/);\n    if (dpkg) {\n      // Strip epoch (e.g. \"2:21.1.12-1ubuntu1.5\" -> \"21.1.12-1ubuntu1.5\")\n      // Then strip distro suffix (e.g. \"21.1.12-1ubuntu1.5\" -> \"21.1.12\")\n      const ver = dpkg[1].replace(/^\\d+:/, '').replace(/-.*$/, '');\n      return { version: ver, extra: [] };\n    }\n    // X.Org X Server version output (if it ever works)\n    const xorg = raw.match(/X\\.Org\\s+X\\s+Server\\s+([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (xorg) return { version: xorg[1], extra: [] };\n    return null;\n  },\n  // Xvfb returns \"Unrecognized option: -version\" — this is handled by fixing the command\n  // to use dpkg fallback first\n\n  // agent 1.0.0 or similar\n  agent: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // codex-cli 0.117.0 or similar\n  codex: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // opencode 1.3.10 or similar\n  opencode: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // qwen-code version\n  qwenCode: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // gemini version\n  gemini: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n};\n\n/**\n * Parse a raw version string using the per-tool parser, returning uniform format:\n *   \"<version>\" or \"<version> (<extra1>, <extra2>, ...)\"\n * Falls back to the raw string if no parser matches.\n * @param {string} key - Tool key (must match a key in VERSION_PARSERS)\n * @param {string} raw - Raw version string from command output\n * @returns {string} Parsed version string in uniform format\n */\nexport function parseVersion(key, raw) {\n  if (!raw) return raw;\n  const parser = VERSION_PARSERS[key];\n  if (!parser) return raw;\n  const result = parser(raw);\n  if (!result) return raw;\n  const { version, extra } = result;\n  if (extra && extra.length > 0) {\n    return `${version} (${extra.join(', ')})`;\n  }\n  return version;\n}\n\n/**\n * Command definitions for version checking\n * Each entry has: key, command, and optional fallbacks\n * @type {Array<{key: string, command: string, fallbacks?: string[]}>}\n */\nconst VERSION_COMMANDS = [\n  // AI Agents and Tools (--tool options)\n  { key: 'claudeCode', command: 'claude --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'agent', command: 'agent --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'codex', command: 'codex --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'opencode', command: 'opencode --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'qwenCode', command: 'qwen-code --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'gemini', command: 'gemini --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'copilot', command: 'copilot --version 2>&1' },\n\n  // Browser Automation\n  { key: 'playwright', command: 'playwright --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'playwrightTest', command: \"npm list -g @playwright/test --depth=0 2>&1 | grep @playwright/test | awk '{print $2}'\" },\n  { key: 'playwrightMcp', command: \"npm list -g @playwright/mcp --depth=0 2>&1 | grep @playwright/mcp | awk '{print $2}'\" },\n  { key: 'playwrightMcpStatus', command: 'timeout 5 claude mcp list 2>&1 | grep -i playwright | key: 'chrome', command: 'google-chrome --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'chromium', command: 'chromium --version 2>&1', fallbacks: ['chromium-browser --version 2>&1', \"ls ~/.cache/ms-playwright/ 'pyenv --version 2>&1' },\n\n  // Rust ecosystem\n  { key: 'rust', command: 'rustc --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'cargo', command: 'cargo --version 2>&1' },\n\n  // Java ecosystem\n  { key: 'java', command: 'java -version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'sdkman', command: \"sdk version 2>&1 | grep -oE '[0-9]+\\\\.[0-9]+\\\\.[0-9]+'\" },\n\n  // Go\n  { key: 'go', command: 'go version 2>&1' },\n\n  // PHP\n  { key: 'php', command: 'php --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n\n  // .NET\n  { key: 'dotnet', command: 'dotnet --version 2>&1' },\n\n  // Perl ecosystem\n  { key: 'perl', command: \"perl -v 2>&1 | grep -oE 'v[0-9]+\\\\.[0-9]+\\\\.[0-9]+'\" },\n  { key: 'perlbrew', command: 'perlbrew --version 2>&1' },\n\n  // OCaml/Rocq ecosystem\n  { key: 'ocaml', command: 'ocaml --version 2>&1' },\n  { 'lake --version 2>&1' },\n\n  // C/C++ Development Tools\n  { key: 'gcc', command: 'gcc --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'gpp', command: 'g++ --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'clang', --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'rbenv', command: 'rbenv --version 2>&1' },\n\n  // Kotlin\n  { key: 'kotlin', command: 'kotlin -version 2>&1' },\n\n  // Swift\n  { key: 'swift', command: 'swift --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n\n  // R\n  { key: 'r', command: 'R --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n\n  // Development Tools\n  { key: 'git', command: 'git --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'gh', command: 'gh --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'glab', command: 'glab --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'brew', command: 'brew --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'nasm', command: 'nasm --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'fasm', command: 'fasm 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'curl', command: 'curl --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'wget', command: 'wget --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'zip', command: 'zip --version 2>&1 | head -n2 | tail -n1' },\n  { key: 'unzip', command: 'unzip -v 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'expect', command: 'expect -version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'screen', command: 'screen --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'xvfb', command: 'dpkg -l xvfb 2>/dev/null | grep \"^ii\" | head -1', fallbacks: ['Xvfb -version 2>&1 | head -n1'] },\n];\n\n/**\n * Execute a version command with optional fallbacks\n * @param {{key: string, command: string, fallbacks?: string[]}} cmdDef - Command definition\n * @param {boolean} verbose - Enable verbose logging\n * @returns {Promise<{key: string, value: string|null}>}\n */\nasync function executeVersionCommand(cmdDef, verbose) {\n  let result = await execCommandAsync(cmdDef.command);\n\n  // Try fallbacks if primary command failed\n  if (!result && cmdDef.fallbacks) {\n    for (const fallback of cmdDef.fallbacks) {\n      result = await execCommandAsync(fallback);\n      if (result) break;\n    }\n  }\n\n  if (verbose && result) {\n    console.log(`[VERBOSE] ${cmdDef.key}: ${result}`);\n  } else if (verbose && !result) {\n    console.log(`[VERBOSE] ${cmdDef.key}: not found`);\n  }\n\n  return { key: cmdDef.key, value: result };\n}\n\n/**\n * Get comprehensive version information for all components\n * Uses Promise.all for parallel execution (issue #1320)\n * @param {boolean} verbose - Enable verbose logging\n * @param {string} [processVersion] - Optional: version from the running process (for restart warning)\n * @returns {Promise<Object>} Version information object\n */\nexport async function getVersionInfo(verbose = false, processVersion = null) {\n  const startTime = Date.now();\n\n  try {\n    if (verbose) {\n      console.log('[VERBOSE] Gathering version information (parallel Convert results array to object\n    const versions = {};\n    for (const { key, value } of results) {\n      versions[key] = value;\n    }\n\n    // Add Node.js version (always available from process.platform;\n    const arch = process.arch;\n    versions.platform = `${platform} (${arch})`;\n    if (verbose) {\n      console.log(`[VERBOSE] Platform: ${versions.platform}`);\n    }\n\n    // optimization (issue #1320):\n// Uses Promise.all for parallel command execution instead of sequential execSync.\n// This reduces version gathering time from ~30-150s to ~5s (limited by slowest command).\n\nimport { getVersion } from './version.lib.mjs';\nimport { exec } from 'child_process';\nimport { promisify } from 'util';\n\nconst execAsync = promisify(exec);\n\n/**\n * Month name/abbreviation to zero-padded number mapping\n */\nconst MONTH_MAP = {\n  jan: '01', january: '01',\n  feb: '02', february: '02',\n  mar: '03', march: '03',\n  apr: '04', april: '04',\n  may: '05',\n  jun: '06', june: '06',\n  jul: '07', july: '07',\n  aug: '08', august: '08',\n  sep: '09', september: '09',\n  oct: '10', october: '10',\n  nov: '11', november: '11',\n  dec: '12', december: '12',\n};\n\n/**\n * Normalize a date string to ISO format (YYYY-MM-DD).\n * Handles formats like:\n *   \"20-Aug-23\"       → \"2023-08-20\"\n *   \"20 April 2009\"   → \"2009-04-20\"\n *   \"July 5th 2008\"   → \"2008-07-05\"\n *   \"Jan 13 2026\"     → \"2026-01-13\"\n *   \"2024-02-29\"      → \"2024-02-29\" (passthrough)\n * Returns the original string if parsing fails.\n * @param {string} dateStr - Date string to normalize\n * @returns {string} ISO date string or original\n */\nexport function normalizeDate(dateStr) {\n  if (!dateStr) return dateStr;\n  const s = dateStr.trim();\n\n  // Already ISO: YYYY-MM-DD\n  if (/^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}$/.test(s)) return s;\n\n  // \"DD-Mon-YY\" (e.g. \"20-Aug-23\")\n  const dmy = s.match(/^(\\d{1,2})-([A-Za-z]{3})-(\\d{2})$/);\n  if (dmy) {\n    const month = MONTH_MAP[dmy[2].toLowerCase()];\n    if (month) {\n      const year = parseInt(dmy[3], 10);\n      const fullYear = year >= 70 ? `19${dmy[3]}` : `20${dmy[3]}`;\n      return `${fullYear}-${month}-${dmy[1].padStart(2, '0')}`;\n    }\n  }\n\n  // \"DD Month YYYY\" (e.g. \"20 April 2009\")\n  const dmY = s.match(/^(\\d{1,2})\\s+([A-Za-z]+)\\s+(\\d{4})$/);\n  if (dmY) {\n    const month = MONTH_MAP[dmY[2].toLowerCase()];\n    if (month) return `${dmY[3]}-${month}-${dmY[1].padStart(2, '0')}`;\n  }\n\n  // \"Month DDth YYYY\" or \"Month DD YYYY\" (e.g. \"July 5th 2008\", \"Jan 13 2026\")\n  const mdY = s.match(/^([A-Za-z]+)\\s+(\\d{1,2})(?:st|nd|rd|th)?\\s+(\\d{4})$/);\n  if (mdY) {\n    const month = MONTH_MAP[mdY[1].toLowerCase()];\n    if (month) return `${mdY[3]}-${month}-${mdY[2].padStart(2, '0')}`;\n  }\n\n  // \"Month DD YYYY HH:MM:SS\" (e.g. \"Jan 13 2026 22:36:55\")\n  const mdYt = s.match(/^([A-Za-z]+)\\s+(\\d{1,2})\\s+(\\d{4})\\s+(\\d{2}:\\d{2}:\\d{2})$/);\n  if (mdYt) {\n    const month = MONTH_MAP[mdYt[1].toLowerCase()];\n    if (month) return `${mdYt[3]}-${month}-${mdYt[2].padStart(2, '0')} ${mdYt[4]}`;\n  }\n\n  return s;\n}\n\n/**\n * Strip OS/architecture info from a version extra string.\n * Removes patterns like \"x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu\", \"linux/amd64\", \"[x86_64-linux]\", etc.\n * @param {string} s - String to clean\n * @returns {string} Cleaned string\n */\nfunction stripArchInfo(s) {\n  if (!s) return s;\n  return s\n    // Remove bracketed arch like [x86_64-linux]\n    .replace(/\\[[\\w-]+\\]\\s*/g, '')\n    // Remove triple-target patterns like x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu, x86_64-pc-linux-gnu\n    .replace(/\\b\\w+[-_]\\w+[-_]\\w+[-_]\\w+\\b/g, '')\n    // Remove platform/arch patterns like linux/amd64\n    .replace(/\\b(?:linux|darwin|win32|windows)\\/\\w+\\b/gi, '')\n    .trim();\n}\n\n/**\n * Execute a command asynchronously and return its output, or null if it fails\n * @param {string} command - Command to execute\n * @param {number} timeout - Timeout in milliseconds (default: 5000ms)\n * @returns {Promise<string|null>} Command output or null\n */\nasync function execCommandAsync(command, timeout = 5000) {\n  try {\n    const { stdout } = await execAsync(command, { timeout, maxBuffer: 1024 * 1024 });\n    const trimmed = stdout.trim();\n    // Return null if the output looks like an error message\n    if (trimmed.includes('not found') || trimmed.includes('command not found') || trimmed === '') {\n      return null;\n    }\n    return trimmed;\n  } catch {\n    return null;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Per-tool regex parsers to normalize raw --version output into uniform format:\n *   <version> (<commit>, <revision>, <date>, etc.)\n *\n * Each parser returns { version, extra[] } or null if it doesn't match.\n * The `version` is the most specific version string (for bug reporting).\n * Items in `extra` are joined with \", \" and placed in parentheses.\n *\n * @type {Record<string, (raw: string) => {version: string, extra: string[]} | null>}\n */\nconst VERSION_PARSERS = {\n  // rustc 1.94.1 (e408947bf 2026-03-25)\n  rust: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^rustc\\s+([\\d.]+(?:-\\S+)?)\\s*(?:\\(([^)]+)\\))?/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    const extra = m[2] ? m[2].trim().split(/\\s+/) : [];\n    return { version: m[1], extra };\n  },\n  // cargo 1.94.1 (29ea6fb6a 2026-03-24)\n  cargo: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^cargo\\s+([\\d.]+(?:-\\S+)?)\\s*(?:\\(([^)]+)\\))?/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    const extra = m[2] ? m[2].trim().split(/\\s+/) : [];\n    return { version: m[1], extra };\n  },\n  // go version go1.26.1 linux/amd64 → strip platform/arch\n  go: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/go([\\d.]+(?:\\S*)?)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // PHP 8.3.30 (cli) (built: Jan 13 2026 22:36:55) (NTS) → strip cli, normalize date\n  php: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^PHP\\s+([\\d.]+(?:-\\S+)?)\\s*(.*)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    const tags = [];\n    const parts = m[2].matchAll(/\\(([^)]+)\\)/g);\n    for (const p of parts) {\n      const tag = p[1].trim();\n      // Skip \"cli\" — not meaningful for version display\n      if (tag === 'cli') continue;\n      // Normalize \"built: Jan 13 2026 22:36:55\" → \"2026-01-13 22:36:55\"\n      const built = tag.match(/^built:\\s+(.+)$/);\n      if (built) {\n        tags.push(normalizeDate(built[1]));\n        continue;\n      }\n      tags.push(tag);\n    }\n    return { version: m[1], extra: tags };\n  },\n  // openjdk version \"21\" 2023-09-19 LTS\n  java: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/version\\s+\"([^\"]+)\"(?:\\s+(.+))?/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: m[2] ? [m[2].trim()] : [] };\n  },\n  // gcc (Ubuntu 13.3.0-6ubuntu2~24.04.1) 13.3.0 → use base version only\n  gcc: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^gcc\\s+(?:\\([^)]*\\)\\s+)?([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // g++ (Ubuntu 13.3.0-6ubuntu2~24.04.1) 13.3.0 → use base version only\n  gpp: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^g\\+\\+\\s+(?:\\([^)]*\\)\\s+)?([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // clang version 17.0.0 (https://github.com/... 2e6139970eda) → strip URL, keep commit\n  clang: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^clang\\s+version\\s+([\\d.]+(?:-\\S+)?)\\s*(?:\\(([^)]+)\\))?/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    if (m[2]) {\n      // Remove URLs, keep only hex commit hashes\n      const parts = m[2].trim().split(/\\s+/).filter(p => !p.includes('://') && !p.includes('.git'));\n      const commitParts = parts.filter(p => /^[0-9a-f]{7,}$/i.test(p));\n      return { version: m[1], extra: commitParts };\n    }\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // LLD 17.0.0 (compatible with GNU linkers) — only version number matters\n  lld: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^LLD\\s+([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // Python 3.14.3\n  python: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^Python\\s+([\\d.]+(?:\\S*)?)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // ruby 3.4.9 (2026-03-11 revision 76cca827ab) +PRISM [x86_64-linux] → strip arch, reformat\n  ruby: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^ruby\\s+([\\d.]+(?:p\\d+)?)\\s*(?:\\(([^)]+)\\))?\\s*(.*)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    const extra = [];\n    if (m[2]) {\n      // Parse \"2026-03-11 revision 76cca827ab\" → commit, date\n      const revMatch = m[2].match(/^(\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2})\\s+revision\\s+(\\w+)$/);\n      if (revMatch) {\n        extra.push(revMatch[2]); // commit first\n        extra.push(revMatch[1]); // then date\n      } else {\n        extra.push(m[2].trim());\n      }\n    }\n    // Capture +PRISM or similar flags, but strip [arch] info\n    const tail = m[3] ? m[3].trim() : '';\n    if (tail) {\n      const cleaned = tail.replace(/\\[[\\w-]+\\]/g, '').trim();\n      if (cleaned) extra.push(cleaned);\n    }\n    return { version: m[1], extra };\n  },\n  // Kotlin version 2.3.20-release-208 (JRE 21+35-LTS) → strip -release-NNN suffix\n  kotlin: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^Kotlin\\s+version\\s+([\\d.]+)(?:-release-\\d+)?\\s*(?:\\(([^)]+)\\))?/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: m[2] ? [m[2].trim()] : [] };\n  },\n  // Swift version 6.0.3 (swift-6.0.3-RELEASE) → strip redundant release tag\n  swift: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^Swift\\s+version\\s+([\\d.]+(?:\\.\\d+)?)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // R version 4.3.3 (2024-02-29) -- \"Angel Food Cake\" → normalize date\n  r: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^R\\s+version\\s+([\\d.]+)\\s*(?:\\(([^)]+)\\))?(?:\\s+--\\s+\"([^\"]+)\")?/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    const extra = [];\n    if (m[2]) extra.push(normalizeDate(m[2]));\n    if (m[3]) extra.push(m[3]);\n    return { version: m[1], extra };\n  },\n  // git version 2.43.0\n  git: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^git\\s+version\\s+([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // gh version 2.89.0 (2026-03-26)\n  gh: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^gh\\s+version\\s+([\\d.]+)\\s*(?:\\(([^)]+)\\))?/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: m[2] ? [m[2]] : [] };\n  },\n  // glab version 1.36.0\n  glab: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^glab\\s+version\\s+([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // curl 8.19.0 (x86_64-pc-linux-gnu) libcurl/8.19.0 ... → strip arch info\n  curl: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^curl\\s+([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // GNU Wget 1.21.4 built on linux-gnu.\n  wget: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^GNU\\s+Wget\\s+([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // cmake version 3.28.3\n  cmake: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^cmake\\s+version\\s+([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // GNU {\n    const m = raw.match(/^NASM\\s+version\\s+([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // flat assembler  version 1.73.32\n  fasm: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/version\\s+([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // Screen version 4.09.01 (GNU) 20-Aug-23 → normalize date, strip GNU\n  screen: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^Screen\\s+version\\s+([\\d.]+)\\s*(?:\\([^)]*\\))?\\s*(.*)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    const extra = [];\n    const dateStr = m[2] ? m[2].trim() : '';\n    if (dateStr) extra.push(normalizeDate(dateStr));\n    return { version: m[1], extra };\n  },\n  // expect version 5.45.4\n  expect: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^expect\\s+version\\s+([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // The OCaml toplevel, version 5.4.1\n  ocaml: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/version\\s+([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // The Rocq Prover, version 9.1.1\n  rocq: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/version\\s+([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // elan 4.2.1 (3d5138e15 2026-03-18)\n  elan: raw => {\n    const m = (version 4.29.0, x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu, commit abc123, Release) → strip arch/Release\n  lean: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/version\\s+([\\d.]+)(?:,\\s*(.+?))\\)?$/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    const extra = m[2]\n      ? m[2]\n          .split(',')\n          .map(s => s.trim().replace(/\\)$/, ''))\n          .filter(s => {\n            if (!s) return false;\n            // Strip [];\n    return { version: m[1], extra };\n  },\n  // Google Chrome 146.0.7680.164\n  chrome: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^Google\\s+Chrome\\s+([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // Chromium 137.0.7151.0\n  chromium: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^Chromium\\s+([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // Mozilla Firefox 139.0\n  firefox: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^Mozilla\\s+Firefox\\s+([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // Microsoft Edge 146.0.3856.84\n  msedge: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^Microsoft\\s+Edge\\s+([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // deno 2.7.9 (stable, release, x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu) → keep only channel (stable)\n  deno: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^deno\\s+([\\d.]+)\\s*(?:\\(([^)]+)\\))?/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    const extra = m[2]\n      ? m[2]\n          .split(',')\n          .map(s => s.trim())\n          .filter(s => s && s !== 'release' && !s.includes('-') && !s.includes('/'))\n      : [];\n    return { m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // @playwright/test@1.58.2\n  playwrightTest: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/@playwright\\/test@([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // @playwright/mcp@0.0.69 or `-- @playwright/mcp@0.0.69\n  playwrightMcp: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/@playwright\\/mcp@([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // @puppeteer/browsers@2.13.0\n  puppeteerBrowsers: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/@puppeteer\\/browsers@([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // 2.1.87 (Claude Code) → strip redundant product name\n  claudeCode: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // GitHub Copilot CLI 1.0.14.\\nRun 'copilot update'...\n  copilot: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    // Strip trailing dot from version (e.g. \"1.0.14.\" -> \"1.0.14\")\n    const version = m[1].replace(/\\.$/, '');\n    return { version, extra: [] };\n  },\n  // pyenv 2.6.26\n  pyenv: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^pyenv\\s+([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // /workspace/.perl5/bin/perlbrew  - App::perlbrew/1.02\n  perlbrew: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/App::perlbrew\\/([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // rbenv 1.3.2-20-g23c3041\n  rbenv: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^rbenv\\s+([\\d.]+(?:-[\\w]+)*)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // Homebrew 5.1.2\n  brew: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/^Homebrew\\s+([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // This is Zip 3.0 (July 5th 2008), by Info-ZIP. → normalize date\n  zip: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/Zip\\s+([\\d.]+)\\s*(?:\\(([^)]+)\\))?/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: m[2] ? [normalizeDate(m[2])] : [] };\n  },\n  // UnZip 6.00 of 20 April 2009, by Debian. → normalize date\n  unzip: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/UnZip\\s+([\\d.]+)\\s*(?:of\\s+([^,]+))?/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: m[2] ? [normalizeDate(m[2].trim())] : [] };\n  },\n  // ii  xvfb  2:21.1.12-1ubuntu1.5  amd64  Virtual Framebuffer... → base version only\n  xvfb: raw => {\n    // dpkg output format\n    const dpkg = raw.match(/^ii\\s+xvfb\\s+(\\S+)/);\n    if (dpkg) {\n      // Strip epoch (e.g. \"2:21.1.12-1ubuntu1.5\" -> \"21.1.12-1ubuntu1.5\")\n      // Then strip distro suffix (e.g. \"21.1.12-1ubuntu1.5\" -> \"21.1.12\")\n      const ver = dpkg[1].replace(/^\\d+:/, '').replace(/-.*$/, '');\n      return { version: ver, extra: [] };\n    }\n    // X.Org X Server version output (if it ever works)\n    const xorg = raw.match(/X\\.Org\\s+X\\s+Server\\s+([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (xorg) return { version: xorg[1], extra: [] };\n    return null;\n  },\n  // Xvfb returns \"Unrecognized option: -version\" — this is handled by fixing the command\n  // to use dpkg fallback first\n\n  // agent 1.0.0 or similar\n  agent: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // codex-cli 0.117.0 or similar\n  codex: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // opencode 1.3.10 or similar\n  opencode: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // qwen-code version\n  qwenCode: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n  // gemini version\n  gemini: raw => {\n    const m = raw.match(/([\\d.]+)/);\n    if (!m) return null;\n    return { version: m[1], extra: [] };\n  },\n};\n\n/**\n * Parse a raw version string using the per-tool parser, returning uniform format:\n *   \"<version>\" or \"<version> (<extra1>, <extra2>, ...)\"\n * Falls back to the raw string if no parser matches.\n * @param {string} key - Tool key (must match a key in VERSION_PARSERS)\n * @param {string} raw - Raw version string from command output\n * @returns {string} Parsed version string in uniform format\n */\nexport function parseVersion(key, raw) {\n  if (!raw) return raw;\n  const parser = VERSION_PARSERS[key];\n  if (!parser) return raw;\n  const result = parser(raw);\n  if (!result) return raw;\n  const { version, extra } = result;\n  if (extra && extra.length > 0) {\n    return `${version} (${extra.join(', ')})`;\n  }\n  return version;\n}\n\n/**\n * Command definitions for version checking\n * Each entry has: key, command, and optional fallbacks\n * @type {Array<{key: string, command: string, fallbacks?: string[]}>}\n */\nconst VERSION_COMMANDS = [\n  // AI Agents and Tools (--tool options)\n  { key: 'claudeCode', command: 'claude --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'agent', command: 'agent --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'codex', command: 'codex --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'opencode', command: 'opencode --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'qwenCode', command: 'qwen-code --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'gemini', command: 'gemini --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'copilot', command: 'copilot --version 2>&1' },\n\n  // Browser Automation\n  { key: 'playwright', command: 'playwright --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'playwrightTest', command: \"npm list -g @playwright/test --depth=0 2>&1 | grep @playwright/test | awk '{print $2}'\" },\n  { key: 'playwrightMcp', command: \"npm list -g @playwright/mcp --depth=0 2>&1 | grep @playwright/mcp | awk '{print $2}'\" },\n  { key: 'playwrightMcpStatus', command: 'timeout 5 claude mcp list 2>&1 | grep -i playwright | head -1' },\n  { key: 'puppeteerBrowsers', command: \"npm list -g @puppeteer/browsers --depth=0 2>&1 | grep @puppeteer/browsers | awk '{print $2}'\" },\n\n  // Browsers (installed via Playwright)\n  { key: 'chrome', command: 'google-chrome --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'chromium', command: 'chromium --version 2>&1', fallbacks: ['chromium-browser --version 2>&1', \"ls ~/.cache/ms-playwright/ 2>/dev/null | grep -oE 'chromium-[0-9]+' | head -1\"] },\n  { key: 'firefox', command: 'firefox --version 2>&1', fallbacks: [\"ls ~/.cache/ms-playwright/ 2>/dev/null | grep -oE 'firefox-[0-9]+' | head -1\"] },\n  { key: 'msedge', command: 'microsoft-edge --version 2>&1', fallbacks: ['microsoft-edge-stable --version 2>&1'] },\n  { key: 'webkit', command: \"ls ~/.cache/ms-playwright/ 2>/dev/null | grep -oE 'webkit-[0-9]+' | head -1\" },\n\n  // JavaScript/Node.js ecosystem\n  { key: 'bun', command: 'bun --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'deno', command: 'deno --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'npm', command: 'npm --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'nvm', command: 'nvm --version 2>&1' },\n\n  // Python ecosystem\n  { key: 'python', command: 'python --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'pyenv', command: 'pyenv --version 2>&1' },\n\n  // Rust ecosystem\n  { key: 'rust', command: 'rustc --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'cargo', command: 'cargo --version 2>&1' },\n\n  // Java ecosystem\n  { key: 'java', command: 'java -version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'sdkman', command: \"sdk version 2>&1 | grep -oE '[0-9]+\\\\.[0-9]+\\\\.[0-9]+'\" },\n\n  // Go\n  { key: 'go', command: 'go version 2>&1' },\n\n  // PHP\n  { key: 'php', command: 'php --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n\n  // .NET\n  { key: 'dotnet', command: 'dotnet --version 2>&1' },\n\n  // Perl ecosystem\n  { key: 'perl', command: \"perl -v 2>&1 | grep -oE 'v[0-9]+\\\\.[0-9]+\\\\.[0-9]+'\" },\n  { key: 'perlbrew', command: 'perlbrew --version 2>&1' },\n\n  // OCaml/Rocq ecosystem\n  { key: 'ocaml', command: 'ocaml --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'opam', command: 'opam --version 2>&1' },\n  // Rocq has fallback commands (rocq -> rocqc -> coqc)\n  { key: 'rocq', command: 'rocq -v 2>&1 | head -n1', fallbacks: ['rocqc --version 2>&1 | head -n1', 'coqc --version 2>&1 | head -n1'] },\n\n  // Lean ecosystem\n  { key: 'lean', command: 'lean --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'elan', command: 'elan --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'lake', command: 'lake --version 2>&1' },\n\n  // C/C++ Development Tools\n  { key: 'gcc', command: 'gcc --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'gpp', command: 'g++ --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'clang', command: 'clang --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'llvm', command: 'llvm-config --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'lld', command: 'ld.lld --version 2>&1 | head -n1', fallbacks: ['lld --version 2>&1 | --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'rbenv', command: 'rbenv --version 2>&1' },\n\n  // Kotlin\n  { key: 'kotlin', command: 'kotlin -version 2>&1' },\n\n  // Swift\n  { key: 'swift', command: 'swift --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n\n  // R\n  { key: 'r', command: 'R --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n\n  // Development Tools\n  { key: 'git', command: 'git --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'gh', command: 'gh --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'glab', command: 'glab --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'brew', command: 'brew --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'nasm', command: 'nasm --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'fasm', command: 'fasm 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'curl', command: 'curl --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'wget', command: 'wget --version 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'zip', command: 'zip --version 2>&1 | head -n2 | tail -n1' },\n  { key: 'unzip', command: 'unzip -v 2>&1 | head -n1' },\n  { key: 'expect', command: 'expect -version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'screen', command: 'screen --version 2>&1' },\n  { key: 'xvfb', command: 'dpkg -l xvfb 2>/dev/null | grep \"^ii\" | head -1', fallbacks: ['Xvfb -version 2>&1 | head -n1'] },\n];\n\n/**\n * Execute a version command with optional fallbacks\n * @param {{key: string, command: string, fallbacks?: string[]}} cmdDef - Command definition\n * @param {boolean} verbose - Enable verbose logging\n * @returns {Promise<{key: string, value: string|null}>}\n */\nasync function executeVersionCommand(cmdDef, verbose) {\n  let result = await execCommandAsync(cmdDef.command);\n\n  // Try fallbacks if primary command failed\n  if (!result && cmdDef.fallbacks) {\n    for (const fallback of cmdDef.fallbacks) {\n      result = await execCommandAsync(fallback);\n      if (result) break;\n    }\n  }\n\n  if (verbose && result) {\n    console.log(`[VERBOSE] ${cmdDef.key}: ${result}`);\n  } else if (verbose && !result) {\n    console.log(`[VERBOSE] ${cmdDef.key}: not found`);\n  }\n\n  return { key: cmdDef.key, value: result };\n}\n\n/**\n * Get comprehensive version information for all components\n * Uses Promise.all for parallel execution (issue #1320)\n * @param {boolean} verbose - Enable verbose logging\n * @param {string} [processVersion] - Optional: version from the running process (for restart warning)\n * @returns {Promise<Object>} Version information object\n */\nexport async function getVersionInfo(verbose = false, processVersion = null) {\n  const startTime = Date.now();\n\n  try {\n    if (verbose) {\n      console.log('[VERBOSE] Gathering version information (parallel execution)...');\n    }\n\n    // Get hive-mind package version\n    const packageVersion = await getVersion();\n    if (verbose) {\n      console.log(`[VERBOSE] Package version: ${packageVersion}`);\n    }\n\n    // Execute all version commands in parallel\n    const results = await Promise.all(VERSION_COMMANDS.map(cmd => executeVersionCommand(cmd, verbose)));\n\n    // Convert results array to object\n    const versions = {};\n    for (const { key, value } of results) {\n      versions[key] = value;\n    }\n\n    // Add Node.js version (always available from process)\n    versions.node = process.version;\n    if (verbose) {\n      console.log(`[VERBOSE] Node.js version: ${versions.node}`);\n    }\n\n    // Platform information\n    const platform = process.platform;\n    const arch = process.arch;\n    versions.platform = `${platform} (${arch})`;\n    if (verbose) {\n      console.log(`[VERBOSE] Platform: ${versions.platform}`);\n    }\n\n    // Check if process version differs from installed version (restart warning)\n    const needsRestart = processVersion && processVersion !== packageVersion;\n\n    // Build version info object\n    const
